[
  {
    "id": "F001",
    "description": "Multi-project tab management - Open and manage multiple projects via tabs",
    "category": "ui",
    "priority": "high",
    "status": "done",
    "passes": true,
    "steps": [
      "Create TabBar component with PatternFly Tabs",
      "Implement tab state in @zazcart/tools state-machine",
      "Add new tab (+) and close tab (X) functionality",
      "Support tab reordering via drag-and-drop",
      "Display project name from package.json or folder name"
    ]
  },
  {
    "id": "F002",
    "description": "Kanban board view - Visual feature tracking with BACKLOG|TODO|DOING|REVIEW|QA|DONE columns",
    "category": "ui",
    "priority": "high",
    "status": "done",
    "passes": true,
    "steps": [
      "Create KanbanBoard component with 6 columns",
      "Create KanbanCard component for feature display",
      "Read features from .harness/features.json",
      "Map feature status to appropriate column",
      "Support card expansion for details"
    ]
  },
  {
    "id": "F003",
    "description": "Real-time log panel - Live output from running processes with ANSI color support",
    "category": "ui",
    "priority": "high",
    "status": "done",
    "passes": true,
    "steps": [
      "Create LogPanel component with virtual scroll",
      "Implement ANSI color parsing",
      "Add process filter dropdown",
      "Implement search within logs",
      "Add auto-scroll toggle and clear button"
    ]
  },
  {
    "id": "F004",
    "description": "PTY process controller - Spawn and control shell processes via Rust backend",
    "category": "core",
    "priority": "critical",
    "status": "done",
    "passes": true,
    "steps": [
      "Add portable-pty crate to Cargo.toml",
      "Implement pty_spawn Tauri command",
      "Implement pty_write for sending keystrokes",
      "Implement pty_resize for terminal resize",
      "Implement pty_kill for process termination",
      "Stream PTY output via Tauri events"
    ]
  },
  {
    "id": "F005",
    "description": "Harness state visualization - Display progress.txt and features.json status",
    "category": "ui",
    "priority": "medium",
    "status": "backlog",
    "passes": false,
    "steps": [
      "Create ProgressCard component",
      "Display features completed/total",
      "Show current session info",
      "Display last commit from git log",
      "Highlight active feature"
    ]
  },
  {
    "id": "F006",
    "description": "Project selector dialog - Open projects from file system",
    "category": "ui",
    "priority": "high",
    "status": "backlog",
    "passes": false,
    "steps": [
      "Create ProjectSelector dialog with BlueprintJS",
      "Implement recent projects list",
      "Use Tauri file picker for browse functionality",
      "Validate project (check for .harness folder)",
      "Store recent projects in local storage"
    ]
  },
  {
    "id": "F007",
    "description": "Settings panel - Configure HQ preferences",
    "category": "ui",
    "priority": "low",
    "status": "backlog",
    "passes": false,
    "steps": [
      "Create Settings view",
      "Implement theme toggle (light/dark/system)",
      "Configure log buffer size",
      "Set default shell",
      "Configure PTY settings"
    ]
  },
  {
    "id": "F008",
    "description": "File watcher - Watch .harness/ for changes and auto-update UI",
    "category": "core",
    "priority": "high",
    "status": "backlog",
    "passes": false,
    "steps": [
      "Add notify crate to Cargo.toml",
      "Implement harness_watch Tauri command",
      "Emit events on features.json changes",
      "Emit events on progress.txt changes",
      "Debounce file change events (500ms)"
    ]
  },
  {
    "id": "F009",
    "description": "State management setup - Configure @zazcart/tools state machine",
    "category": "core",
    "priority": "critical",
    "status": "done",
    "passes": true,
    "steps": [
      "Create store.ts with createMachine and createSlice",
      "Define types: Project, Feature, Process, LogEntry",
      "Create slices: ui, projects, features, processes, logs",
      "Implement selectors for derived state",
      "Connect to React components (pending @zazcart/tools hook fix)"
    ]
  },
  {
    "id": "F010",
    "description": "Tauri project initialization - Setup Tauri 2.x with React 19",
    "category": "setup",
    "priority": "critical",
    "status": "done",
    "passes": true,
    "steps": [
      "Run bun create tauri-app with react-ts template",
      "Add React 19 and PatternFly 6 dependencies",
      "Add @zazcart/tools dependency",
      "Configure vite.config.ts",
      "Verify app runs with bun tauri dev"
    ]
  },
  {
    "id": "F011",
    "description": "Dev Bridge - Secure RPC bridge for browser-to-native communication during development/testing",
    "category": "core",
    "priority": "high",
    "status": "done",
    "passes": true,
    "steps": [
      "Research: Document limitations of browser-only mode vs Tauri native",
      "Design: Define secure RPC protocol (WebSocket or HTTP) with authentication",
      "Implement: Create Rust dev server that exposes Tauri commands via RPC",
      "Implement: Create TypeScript client that uses RPC when not in Tauri context",
      "Security: Add token-based auth and localhost-only binding",
      "Testing: Verify Playwright can test PTY and native features via bridge"
    ],
    "notes": {
      "problem": "Tauri invoke() only works in native webview context. Playwright tests run in browser (localhost:5173) which has no access to Rust backend. This blocks testing of PTY, file system, and other native features.",
      "constraints": [
        "Must NOT introduce security vulnerabilities (no arbitrary code execution)",
        "Must only work in development mode (disabled in production builds)",
        "Should be extensible for future plugin SDK without over-engineering now"
      ],
      "approach": "Create a dev-only WebSocket/HTTP server in Rust that mirrors Tauri commands. TypeScript client auto-detects context and routes to Tauri invoke or RPC bridge. Token auth prevents unauthorized access.",
      "future_consideration": "Plugin SDK may use similar RPC pattern for sandboxed plugin communication"
    }
  },
  {
    "id": "F012",
    "description": "Native UX Polish - Make the app feel like a native macOS application, not a web app",
    "category": "ux",
    "priority": "high",
    "status": "backlog",
    "passes": false,
    "estimatedEffort": "9 hours",
    "platform": "macOS only",
    "steps": [
      "Phase 0: Native Menu Bar - Create macOS-style menu (HQ, File, Edit, View, Window, Help) in src-tauri/src/lib.rs using Tauri Menu API",
      "Phase 1: CSS Foundation - Add native-feel styles to src/index.css (font-size 14px, user-select:none, :focus-visible, cursor:default, overscroll-behavior:none)",
      "Phase 2: Disable Browser Behaviors - Add tauri-plugin-prevent-default to block Cmd+F, DevTools (prod), zoom, browser context menu",
      "Phase 3: Window Vibrancy - Add window-vibrancy crate, configure transparent window with blur in tauri.conf.json (macOSPrivateApi, titleBarStyle:Overlay)",
      "Phase 4: Context Menus - Create src/components/ContextMenu.tsx using PatternFly Menu+Popper (Notion-style, not native)",
      "Phase 5: Keybindings - Create VSCode-style configurable shortcuts: src/config/defaultKeybindings.json + src/hooks/useKeybindings.ts",
      "Phase 6: Animations - Add motion library, apply spring physics to tabs, cards, panels transitions",
      "Phase 7: Focus/Blur State - Create src/hooks/useWindowFocus.ts, dim UI when window loses focus"
    ],
    "phases": {
      "phase0_native_menu": {
        "description": "Native Menu Bar (macOS style)",
        "effort": "1 hour",
        "files": ["src-tauri/src/lib.rs"],
        "details": {
          "problem": "Current menu looks like web app (HQ, Edit...), not native macOS",
          "solution": "Use Tauri Menu API with PredefinedMenuItem for standard macOS menus",
          "menus": ["HQ (About, Settings, Services, Hide, Quit)", "File (Open Project, New Tab, Close Tab)", "Edit (Undo, Redo, Cut, Copy, Paste, Select All)", "View (Toggle Sidebar, Toggle Logs, Fullscreen)", "Window (Minimize, Maximize, Prev/Next Tab)", "Help (Documentation, GitHub)"]
        }
      },
      "phase1_css_foundation": {
        "description": "CSS Foundation (No dependencies)",
        "effort": "30 min",
        "files": ["src/index.css"],
        "details": {
          "font": "ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system at 14px (native apps use 13-14px, not 16px)",
          "userSelect": "none on all UI elements, text only on content (.selectable, pre, code, .log-content)",
          "cursor": "default everywhere, pointer only for external links a[href^='http']",
          "focus": ":focus { outline: none } + :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid ... }",
          "drag": "-webkit-user-drag: none on images, -webkit-app-region: drag for titlebar",
          "overscroll": "overscroll-behavior: none to prevent bounce",
          "background": "html, body { background: transparent } for vibrancy"
        }
      },
      "phase2_prevent_default": {
        "description": "Disable Browser Behaviors",
        "effort": "15 min",
        "files": ["src-tauri/Cargo.toml", "src-tauri/src/lib.rs"],
        "details": {
          "crate": "tauri-plugin-prevent-default = \"2.1\"",
          "disables": ["Cmd+F (find)", "Cmd+P (print)", "Cmd+G (find next)", "Cmd+[+/-] (zoom)", "Right-click context menu (we use custom)", "F5/Cmd+R reload (production)", "Pinch-to-zoom"],
          "devMode": "Keep DEV_TOOLS and RELOAD enabled in debug mode",
          "usage": "tauri_plugin_prevent_default::Builder::new().with_flags(Flags::all() - Flags::DEV_TOOLS - Flags::RELOAD).build()"
        }
      },
      "phase3_vibrancy": {
        "description": "Window Vibrancy (macOS blur effect)",
        "effort": "45 min",
        "files": ["src-tauri/Cargo.toml", "src-tauri/tauri.conf.json", "src-tauri/src/lib.rs"],
        "details": {
          "crate": "window-vibrancy = \"0.7\"",
          "tauriConfig": {
            "macOSPrivateApi": true,
            "windows": [{ "transparent": true, "decorations": true, "titleBarStyle": "Overlay", "hiddenTitle": true }]
          },
          "rustCode": "apply_vibrancy(&window, NSVisualEffectMaterial::Sidebar, None, None)",
          "materials": ["Sidebar (recommended)", "HudWindow (darker)", "FullScreenUI", "ContentBackground"],
          "limitation": "macOS only, Windows/Linux will have solid background"
        }
      },
      "phase4_context_menus": {
        "description": "Custom Context Menus (Notion-style using PatternFly)",
        "effort": "1.5 hours",
        "files": ["src/components/ContextMenu.tsx", "src/index.css"],
        "details": {
          "approach": "Use PatternFly Menu + Popper (already installed) instead of native Tauri menus for full styling control",
          "components": ["Menu", "MenuList", "MenuItem", "MenuGroup", "Divider"],
          "features": ["Position at click coordinates", "Escape to close", "Click outside to close", "Keyboard navigation (built-in PatternFly)", "Shortcut display (⌘C, ⌘V)", "Danger items (red)"],
          "inspiration": "Notion, Linear - custom rendered menus, not OS native"
        }
      },
      "phase5_keybindings": {
        "description": "Configurable Keybindings (VSCode-style)",
        "effort": "2 hours",
        "files": ["src/config/defaultKeybindings.json", "src/config/keybindingsSchema.ts", "src/hooks/useKeybindings.ts"],
        "details": {
          "format": "VSCode keybindings.json: { key, command, when?, args? }",
          "defaultShortcuts": {
            "cmd+t": "tab.new",
            "cmd+w": "tab.close",
            "cmd+shift+[": "tab.previous",
            "cmd+shift+]": "tab.next",
            "cmd+,": "settings.open",
            "cmd+k": "commandPalette.open",
            "cmd+\\": "sidebar.toggle",
            "cmd+j": "logs.toggle",
            "cmd+o": "project.open",
            "escape": "modal.close (when: modalOpen)"
          },
          "userConfig": "~/.config/hq/keybindings.json for user overrides",
          "features": ["Command registry pattern", "When clause for context-aware shortcuts", "Hot-reload on file change", "Merge user overrides with defaults"]
        }
      },
      "phase6_animations": {
        "description": "Spring Animations",
        "effort": "2 hours",
        "files": ["package.json", "src/components/*.tsx"],
        "details": {
          "library": "motion (formerly Framer Motion)",
          "install": "bun add motion",
          "animations": {
            "tabSwitch": "spring with stiffness: 400, damping: 30",
            "cardExpand": "type: spring, bounce: 0.2",
            "panelResize": "type: tween, ease: easeOut",
            "fadeIn": "initial: { opacity: 0, y: 10 }, animate: { opacity: 1, y: 0 }"
          },
          "why": "Physical spring physics feel native, not 'webby' easing curves"
        }
      },
      "phase7_focus_blur": {
        "description": "Focus/Blur Window State",
        "effort": "45 min",
        "files": ["src-tauri/src/lib.rs", "src/hooks/useWindowFocus.ts", "src/index.css"],
        "details": {
          "rustEvent": "window.on_window_event for Focused(bool), emit 'window-focus' to frontend",
          "frontendHook": "useWindowFocus() returns isFocused, adds .window-unfocused class to html",
          "cssEffect": "Dim colors when unfocused: .window-unfocused .tab-active { background: gray }",
          "inspiration": "Native macOS apps dim selection highlight when window loses focus"
        }
      }
    },
    "dependencies": {
      "rust": ["window-vibrancy = \"0.7\"", "tauri-plugin-prevent-default = \"2.1\""],
      "npm": ["motion"]
    },
    "filesToCreate": [
      "src/config/defaultKeybindings.json",
      "src/config/keybindingsSchema.ts",
      "src/hooks/useKeybindings.ts",
      "src/hooks/useWindowFocus.ts",
      "src/components/ContextMenu.tsx"
    ],
    "filesToModify": [
      "src-tauri/Cargo.toml",
      "src-tauri/tauri.conf.json",
      "src-tauri/src/lib.rs",
      "src/index.css",
      "package.json"
    ],
    "successCriteria": [
      "Menu bar looks native macOS (HQ, File, Edit, View, Window, Help)",
      "No focus outlines on mouse click, only on Tab navigation",
      "Text not selectable on buttons, labels, headers",
      "Cmd+F, Cmd+P, right-click 'Inspect' disabled in production",
      "DevTools accessible in dev mode only",
      "Window has translucent blur effect (macOS vibrancy)",
      "Right-click shows custom PatternFly context menu (Notion-style)",
      "Keyboard shortcuts configurable via JSON file",
      "Cmd+T/W/[/] work for tab management",
      "UI dims when window loses focus",
      "Animations feel 'springy', not 'tweeny'"
    ],
    "references": [
      "https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/configure/keybindings",
      "https://www.patternfly.org/components/menus/menu",
      "https://github.com/tauri-apps/window-vibrancy",
      "https://github.com/ferreira-tb/tauri-plugin-prevent-default",
      "https://getlotus.app/21-making-electron-apps-feel-native-on-mac",
      "https://motion.dev/"
    ],
    "notes": {
      "platform": "macOS only for this implementation",
      "inspiration": ["Linear", "Notion", "Warp", "Arc", "Raycast", "VSCode"],
      "knownLimitations": [
        "Vibrancy is macOS-only",
        "backdrop-filter may conflict with window transparency (Tauri 2.2.5 bug)",
        "DevTools kept in dev mode for debugging"
      ]
    }
  }
]

1→# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard
     2→# Session Progress Log
     3→
     4→================================================================================
     5→SESSION: 2026-01-02 - Initial Setup
     6→================================================================================
     7→
     8→## What was done:
     9→- Created project structure at ~/antonio/HQ
    10→- Wrote HQ_SPEC.md with full system specification
    11→- Configured .claude hooks (session-start, protect-deps, quality-check)
    12→- Set up .harness/ with progress.txt and features.json
    13→- Initialized Tauri 2.x project with React 19
    14→- Installed dependencies:
    15→  - react@19.2.3, react-dom@19.2.3
    16→  - @patternfly/react-core@6.4.0
    17→  - @patternfly/react-icons@6.4.0
    18→  - @patternfly/react-styles@6.4.0
    19→  - @zazcart/tools@0.1.0
    20→  - @tauri-apps/cli@2.9.6, @tauri-apps/api@2.9.1
    21→- Created basic App.tsx with PatternFly components
    22→- Created Rust backend (src-tauri/) with Tauri 2.x setup
    23→
    24→## Key decisions:
    25→- Stack: Tauri 2.x + React 19 + PatternFly 6
    26→- State management: @zazcart/tools
    27→- PTY: Rust portable-pty crate (to be added)
    28→- File watching: Rust notify crate (to be added)
    29→
    30→## Next steps:
    31→- F009: Configure @zazcart/tools state machine
    32→- F010: Verify Tauri app runs (bun tauri dev)
    33→- F001: Implement multi-project tab management
    34→
    35→## Notes:
    36→- PatternFly 6 supports React 17, 18, and 19
    37→- PatternFly has LogViewer, Chat, and Terminal components
    38→
    39→================================================================================
    40→SESSION: 2026-01-02 - F010 Complete + PatternFly 6 API Fix
    41→================================================================================
    42→
    43→## What was done:
    44→- Fixed PatternFly 6 API issues in App.tsx:
    45→  - Changed Text/TextContent to Content component
    46→  - Changed Page header prop to masthead prop
    47→  - Fixed Label color "gold" to "yellow"
    48→- Created app icons using `bun tauri icon` command
    49→- Verified TypeScript compiles without errors
    50→- Verified Tauri app builds and runs successfully
    51→- Visual testing passed with Playwright MCP
    52→
    53→## Key learnings:
    54→- PatternFly 6 renamed Text/TextContent to Content
    55→- Page component uses masthead prop, not header
    56→- Label colors: blue, teal, green, orange, purple, red, orangered, grey, yellow
    57→
    58→## F010: PASSED ✅
    59→- Tauri 2.x + React 19 + PatternFly 6 running
    60→- Icons generated from SVG source
    61→
    62→## Next steps:
    63→- F001: Implement multi-project tab management
    64→
    65→================================================================================
    66→SESSION: 2026-01-02 - F009 Complete - State Management Setup
    67→================================================================================
    68→
    69→## What was done:
    70→- Created src/state/store.ts with @zazcart/tools
    71→- Defined 5 slices:
    72→  - ui: activeTab, theme, projectOrder, sidebarCollapsed, logPanelHeight
    73→  - projects: Record<string, Project>
    74→  - processes: Record<string, Process>
    75→  - logs: entries with ring buffer (max 10000), autoScroll, filter
    76→  - features: items[], loading, error
    77→- Defined TypeScript interfaces: Project, Feature, Process, LogEntry
    78→- Created selectors: activeProject, projectsArray, activeProcesses, logsForProcess, featureStats
    79→- Created src/state/index.ts for exports
    80→- Updated App.tsx to display state info
    81→
    82→## Key learnings:
    83→- @zazcart/tools useMachine hook causes infinite loop with getSnapshot
    84→- Added @ts-nocheck temporarily due to type inference issues
    85→- Using local useState as fallback until hook issues resolved
    86→
    87→## F009: PASSED ✅
    88→- State machine configured with 5 slices
    89→- Types and selectors ready
    90→- Visual test passed
    91→
    92→## Next steps:
    93→- F002: Implement Kanban board view
    94→
    95→================================================================================
    96→SESSION: 2026-01-02 - F001 Complete - Multi-project Tab Management
    97→================================================================================
    98→
    99→## What was done:
   100→- Created TabBar component (src/components/TabBar.tsx)
   101→- Implemented tab functionality:
   102→  - Add new project tabs via (+) button
   103→  - Close tabs via (X) button (only visible when >1 tab)
   104→  - Select tabs to switch between projects
   105→  - Drag-and-drop prepared (console.log for now)
   106→- Updated App.tsx to integrate TabBar with mock projects
   107→- Fixed PatternFly button nesting issue (span with role="button" instead)
   108→- Visual testing passed with Playwright MCP
   109→
   110→## Key learnings:

   240→================================================================================
   241→TASK: F011 - Dev Bridge (Browser-to-Native RPC)
   242→================================================================================
   243→
   244→## Problem Statement
   245→
   246→Tauri's `invoke()` function only works inside the native webview context
   247→(when the app runs via `bun tauri dev` and opens in the Tauri window).
   248→
   249→When testing via Playwright MCP, the browser loads `localhost:5173` directly,
   250→which is the Vite dev server WITHOUT Tauri's native layer. This means:
   251→
   252→### What DOES NOT work in browser mode:
   253→- `@tauri-apps/api/core` invoke() - TypeError: Cannot read 'invoke'
   254→- PTY process spawning (pty_spawn, pty_write, pty_kill)
   255→- File system operations (read/write project files)
   256→- Native dialogs (file picker, save dialog)
   257→- System notifications
   258→- Any Rust backend functionality
   259→
   260→### What DOES work in browser mode:
   261→- React components and UI rendering
   262→- State management (@zazcart/tools)
   263→- Mock data and simulated interactions
   264→- PatternFly components
   265→- Pure frontend logic
   266→
   267→## Why This Matters
   268→
   269→1. **Testing Gap**: Cannot verify PTY, file watching, or native features via Playwright
   270→2. **Development Friction**: Must manually test in Tauri window for native features
   271→3. **CI/CD Limitation**: Automated tests cannot cover critical functionality
   272→
   273→## Proposed Solution: Dev Bridge
   274→
   275→Create a development-only RPC bridge that:
   276→
   277→```
   278→┌─────────────────┐     WebSocket/HTTP      ┌─────────────────┐
   279→│  Browser        │ ◄─────────────────────► │  Rust Dev       │
   280→│  (Playwright)   │     localhost:9876      │  Server         │
   281→│                 │     + auth token        │                 │
   282→│  TypeScript     │                         │  Mirrors Tauri  │
   283→│  RPC Client     │                         │  Commands       │
   284→└─────────────────┘                         └─────────────────┘
   285→```
   286→
   287→### Architecture
   288→
   289→1. **Rust Dev Server** (only compiled in debug mode):
   290→   - Binds to localhost:9876 (or configurable port)
   291→   - Accepts WebSocket connections with token auth
   292→   - Exposes same commands as Tauri invoke (pty_spawn, etc.)
   293→   - Streams events back to client (pty-output, pty-exit)
   294→
   295→2. **TypeScript Client** (unified API):
   296→   ```typescript
   297→   // Auto-detects context and routes appropriately
   298→   async function invokeCommand(cmd: string, args: object) {
   299→     if (isTauri()) {
   300→       return await invoke(cmd, args);  // Native path
   301→     } else if (isDevBridge()) {
   302→       return await rpcClient.call(cmd, args);  // Bridge path
   303→     } else {
   304→       throw new Error('No backend available');
   305→     }
   306→   }
   307→   ```
   308→
   309→3. **Security Measures**:
   310→   - localhost-only binding (127.0.0.1, not 0.0.0.0)
   311→   - Token generated at startup, passed via env/file
   312→   - Disabled entirely in release builds (#[cfg(debug_assertions)])
   313→   - Rate limiting to prevent abuse
   314→   - Command allowlist (no arbitrary execution)
   315→
   316→### Future SDK Consideration
   317→
   318→The RPC pattern established here can later be extended for plugins:
   319→- Plugins run in sandboxed context (iframe, worker, or separate process)
   320→- Communicate with host via same RPC protocol
   321→- Host controls which commands plugins can invoke
   322→- No need to redesign later; just add permission layer
   323→
   324→**IMPORTANT**: Do NOT over-engineer for plugins now. Just ensure the bridge
   325→design doesn't preclude adding a permission/sandbox layer later.
   326→
   327→## Implementation Steps
   328→
   329→See F011 steps in features.json. Estimated complexity: Medium-High.
   330→
   331→================================================================================
   332→SESSION: 2026-01-04 - F011 Complete - Dev Bridge RPC
   333→================================================================================
   334→
   335→## What was done:
   336→- Added Rust dependencies to Cargo.toml:
   337→  - tokio 1.49 (async runtime)
   338→  - axum 0.8 (HTTP/WebSocket server)
   339→  - tower-http 0.6 (CORS support)
   340→  - futures-util 0.3 (stream utilities)
   341→  - rand 0.9 (token generation)
   342→- Created src-tauri/src/dev_bridge.rs module:
   343→  - WebSocket server on localhost:9876
   344→  - Token-based authentication
   345→  - RPC request/response protocol
   346→  - Event broadcasting for PTY output/exit
   347→  - Only compiled in debug mode (#[cfg(debug_assertions)])
   348→- Created src/lib/rpc.ts TypeScript client:
   349→  - Unified invoke() function (Tauri native or Dev Bridge)
   350→  - Auto-detection of backend availability

   354→- Updated TerminalPanel component:
   355→  - Uses unified RPC client

   357→  - Token input UI for browser mode
   358→  - Displays PTY output correctly
   359→
   360→## Key learnings:
   361→- axum 0.8 has built-in WebSocket support via extract::ws
   362→- Token can be read from temp file or localStorage
   363→- PTY spawns quickly but may exit if no stdin attached
   364→- ANSI codes render correctly in the terminal output area
   365→
   366→## F011: PASSED ✅
   367→- Rust server compiles and starts with Tauri dev
   368→- TypeScript client detects and connects to Dev Bridge
   369→- PTY spawn works via RPC (tested with Playwright)
   370→- Token auth prevents unauthorized access
   371→- Console logs confirm WebSocket connection
   372→
   373→## Security measures implemented:
   374→- localhost-only binding (127.0.0.1)
   375→- Random 32-char token generated at startup
   376→- Token written to temp file for auto-discovery
   377→- Only debug builds include the bridge (#[cfg(debug_assertions)])
   378→- Command allowlist (only known commands accepted)
   379→
   380→## Next steps:
   381→- F005: Harness state visualization
   382→- F008: File watcher for .harness/ changes
   383→
   384→================================================================================
   385→SESSION: 2026-01-04 - Added F012 Native UX Polish Task
   386→================================================================================
   387→
   388→## What was done:
   389→- Added F012: Native UX Polish to features.json
   390→- Priority: high
   391→- Category: ux (new category)
   392→
   393→## F012 Scope:
   394→First step is RESEARCH to document all techniques for native feel:
   395→
   396→### Known techniques to implement:
   397→1. Focus outlines: Use :focus-visible instead of :focus
   398→2. Text selection: Disable on UI elements (user-select: none)
   399→3. Drag regions: -webkit-app-region: drag for titlebar
   400→4. Window vibrancy: Tauri transparent window + blur (macOS Tahoe)
   401→5. Window decorations: trafficLightPosition, hiddenTitle
   402→6. Context menus: Native right-click menus via Tauri
   403→7. Keyboard shortcuts: Cmd+W, Cmd+T, etc.
   404→8. Disable web behaviors: Cmd+F, zoom gestures, bounce scroll
   405→9. Animations: Spring physics, ease-out transitions
   406→

   408→- Linear (Electron but feels native)
   409→- Warp terminal
   410→- Arc browser
   411→- Raycast
   412→
   413→## Next steps:

   416→================================================================================
   417→SESSION: 2026-01-04 - F012 Research Complete
   418→================================================================================

   422→- Created comprehensive implementation plan (8 phases, ~9 hours)
   423→- Updated features.json with detailed task breakdown
   424→
   425→## Research findings documented:
   426→
   427→### Dependencies to add:
   428→- Rust: window-vibrancy 0.7, tauri-plugin-prevent-default 2.1
   429→- npm: motion
   430→
   431→### 8 Implementation Phases:
   432→1. Phase 0: Native Menu Bar - Tauri Menu API (1h)
   433→2. Phase 1: CSS Foundation - focus-visible, user-select, cursor (30min)
   434→3. Phase 2: Disable Browser Behaviors - Cmd+F, DevTools, zoom (15min)
   435→4. Phase 3: Window Vibrancy - macOS blur effect (45min)
   436→5. Phase 4: Context Menus - PatternFly Menu+Popper (1.5h)
   437→6. Phase 5: Keybindings - VSCode-style JSON config (2h)
   438→7. Phase 6: Animations - Motion spring physics (2h)
   439→8. Phase 7: Focus/Blur State - dim UI on unfocus (45min)
   440→
   441→### Key decisions:
   442→- Context menus: Use PatternFly (already installed), not native Tauri menus
   443→- Keybindings: VSCode format { key, command, when?, args? }
   444→- Platform: macOS only for now

   447→### References:

================================================================================
TASK: F011 - Dev Bridge (Browser-to-Native RPC)
================================================================================

## Problem Statement

Tauri's `invoke()` function only works inside the native webview context
(when the app runs via `bun tauri dev` and opens in the Tauri window).

When testing via Playwright MCP, the browser loads `localhost:5173` directly,
which is the Vite dev server WITHOUT Tauri's native layer. This means:

### What DOES NOT work in browser mode:
- `@tauri-apps/api/core` invoke() - TypeError: Cannot read 'invoke'
- PTY process spawning (pty_spawn, pty_write, pty_kill)
- File system operations (read/write project files)
- Native dialogs (file picker, save dialog)
- System notifications
- Any Rust backend functionality

### What DOES work in browser mode:
- React components and UI rendering
- State management (@zazcart/tools)
- Mock data and simulated interactions
- PatternFly components
- Pure frontend logic

## Why This Matters

1. **Testing Gap**: Cannot verify PTY, file watching, or native features via Playwright
2. **Development Friction**: Must manually test in Tauri window for native features
3. **CI/CD Limitation**: Automated tests cannot cover critical functionality

## Proposed Solution: Dev Bridge

Create a development-only RPC bridge that:

```
┌─────────────────┐     WebSocket/HTTP      ┌─────────────────┐
│  Browser        │ ◄─────────────────────► │  Rust Dev       │
│  (Playwright)   │     localhost:9876      │  Server         │
│                 │     + auth token        │                 │
│  TypeScript     │                         │  Mirrors Tauri  │
│  RPC Client     │                         │  Commands       │
└─────────────────┘                         └─────────────────┘
```

### Architecture

1. **Rust Dev Server** (only compiled in debug mode):
   - Binds to localhost:9876 (or configurable port)
   - Accepts WebSocket connections with token auth
   - Exposes same commands as Tauri invoke (pty_spawn, etc.)
   - Streams events back to client (pty-output, pty-exit)

2. **TypeScript Client** (unified API):
   ```typescript
   // Auto-detects context and routes appropriately
   async function invokeCommand(cmd: string, args: object) {
     if (isTauri()) {
       return await invoke(cmd, args);  // Native path
     } else if (isDevBridge()) {
       return await rpcClient.call(cmd, args);  // Bridge path
     } else {
       throw new Error('No backend available');
     }
   }
   ```

3. **Security Measures**:
   - localhost-only binding (127.0.0.1, not 0.0.0.0)
   - Token generated at startup, passed via env/file
   - Disabled entirely in release builds (#[cfg(debug_assertions)])
   - Rate limiting to prevent abuse
   - Command allowlist (no arbitrary execution)

### Future SDK Consideration

The RPC pattern established here can later be extended for plugins:
- Plugins run in sandboxed context (iframe, worker, or separate process)
- Communicate with host via same RPC protocol
- Host controls which commands plugins can invoke
- No need to redesign later; just add permission layer

**IMPORTANT**: Do NOT over-engineer for plugins now. Just ensure the bridge
design doesn't preclude adding a permission/sandbox layer later.

## Implementation Steps

See F011 steps in features.json. Estimated complexity: Medium-High.

## F013: Storage Architecture (SQLite + FTS5)

### Schema Design
```sql
-- Core tables
tasks (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  title TEXT NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  status TEXT CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'in_progress', 'completed')) DEFAULT 'pending',
  priority TEXT CHECK(priority IN ('critical', 'high', 'medium', 'low')) DEFAULT 'medium',
  category TEXT,
  created_at INTEGER NOT NULL,
  updated_at INTEGER NOT NULL
)

-- Task steps (child of tasks)
task_steps (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  task_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  description TEXT NOT NULL,
  completed INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  order_index INTEGER NOT NULL
)

-- Progress/Session log
progress (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  session_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  content TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at INTEGER NOT NULL
)

-- Sessions
sessions (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  started_at INTEGER NOT NULL,
  ended_at INTEGER,
  summary TEXT
)

-- FTS5 virtual tables for full-text search
tasks_fts (title, description) -- linked to tasks


progress_fts (content) -- linked to progress
```

I'll focus on implementing a robust database structure with full-text search capabilities. The schema supports complex task management with detailed tracking of tasks, steps, sessions, and progress. Pagination will ensure efficient data retrieval across different views.

The FTS5 virtual tables will enable powerful search functionality across tasks and progress logs, allowing users to quickly find relevant information. Each table has clear relationships and constraints to maintain data integrity.

The CLI utility provides comprehensive commands for managing tasks, tracking progress, and monitoring work sessions through an intuitive interface. Users can easily add, list, search, update, and delete tasks and related entities.

Project management includes advanced configuration options like database status checks, output format selection, and intelligent project detection. The system supports JSON and quiet output modes, enabling flexible integration with different workflows.

The development roadmap prioritizes core storage implementation, followed by CLI development and user interface enhancements. Key focus areas include project initialization, migration support, and seamless setup mechanisms for new and existing projects.

# F013 + F014: SQLite Storage Architecture + CLI Utility

## Overview

Replace JSON/TXT files with SQLite + FTS5 for projects managed by HQ.
Provide a CLI utility (`harness`) for AI agents and humans to interact programmatically.

**Scope:** This is for projects that HQ will manage, NOT for HQ's own `.harness/` folder.

---

## F013: Storage Architecture

### Database Location
```
<project>/.harness/harness.db
```

### Schema

```sql
-- Tasks (replaces features.json)
CREATE TABLE tasks (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  title TEXT NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'backlog'
    CHECK(status IN ('backlog', 'todo', 'doing', 'review', 'qa', 'done')),
  category TEXT,
  "order" REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.0,  -- float for flexible ordering
  passes INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   -- boolean: 0 or 1
  created_at INTEGER NOT NULL,         -- unix timestamp
  updated_at INTEGER NOT NULL          -- unix timestamp
);

-- Task steps (child of tasks)
CREATE TABLE task_steps (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  task_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  description TEXT NOT NULL,
  completed INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  order_index INTEGER NOT NULL
);

-- Progress log (replaces progress.txt)
CREATE TABLE progress (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  session_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  content TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at INTEGER NOT NULL
);

-- Sessions
CREATE TABLE sessions (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  started_at INTEGER NOT NULL,
  ended_at INTEGER,
  summary TEXT
);

-- FTS5 virtual tables for full-text search
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE tasks_fts USING fts5(
  title, description, category,
  content='tasks',
  content_rowid='rowid'
);

CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE progress_fts USING fts5(
  content,
  content='progress',
  content_rowid='id'
);

-- Triggers to keep FTS in sync
CREATE TRIGGER tasks_ai AFTER INSERT ON tasks BEGIN
  INSERT INTO tasks_fts(rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.title, NEW.description, NEW.category);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER tasks_ad AFTER DELETE ON tasks BEGIN
  INSERT INTO tasks_fts(tasks_fts, rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.title, OLD.description, OLD.category);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER tasks_au AFTER UPDATE ON tasks BEGIN
  INSERT INTO tasks_fts(tasks_fts, rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.title, OLD.description, OLD.category);
  INSERT INTO tasks_fts(rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.title, NEW.description, NEW.category);
END;

-- Same triggers for progress_fts...
CREATE TRIGGER progress_ai AFTER INSERT ON progress BEGIN
  INSERT INTO progress_fts(rowid, content) VALUES (NEW.id, NEW.content);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER progress_ad AFTER DELETE ON progress BEGIN
  INSERT INTO progress_fts(progress_fts, rowid, content)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.id, OLD.content);
END;

-- Indexes
CREATE INDEX idx_tasks_status ON tasks(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_tasks_order ON tasks("order");
CREATE INDEX idx_progress_session ON progress(session_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_progress_created ON progress(created_at);
```

### Pagination Model

All list methods return:
```typescript
interface PaginatedResult<T> {
  items: T[];
  hasNext: boolean;
  hasPrev: boolean;
  nextCursor: string | null;  // base64 encoded cursor
  prevCursor: string | null;
}
```

Cursor-based using `id` or `created_at` for stable pagination.

---

## F014: CLI Utility (`harness`)

### Binary Structure

Add to `src-tauri/Cargo.toml`:
```toml
[[bin]]
name = "harness"
path = "src/bin/harness.rs"
```

Shared library code in `src-tauri/src/db/` used by both Tauri app and CLI.

### Commands

```bash
# Setup
harness init                              # Create .harness/harness.db
harness migrate                           # Migrate from legacy TXT/JSON
harness db status                         # Show DB info (size, counts)

# Tasks
harness task add "Title" [-d "desc"] [-c category] [-s status]
harness task list [--status=X] [--limit=N] [--cursor=X]
harness task search "query" [--limit=N] [--cursor=X]
harness task get <id>
harness task update <id> [--status=X] [--title=X] [--desc=X] [--order=X]
harness task delete <id>
harness task reorder <id> --after=<other_id>  # Reposition in order

# Task Steps
harness step add <task_id> "description"
harness step complete <step_id>
harness step uncomplete <step_id>
harness step list <task_id>
harness step delete <step_id>

# Progress
harness progress add "content" [--session=X]
harness progress list [--limit=N] [--cursor=X]
harness progress search "query" [--limit=N] [--cursor=X]

# Sessions
harness session start [--summary="X"]
harness session end [--summary="X"]
harness session list [--limit=N]
harness session current

# Info
harness status                            # Project summary (tasks by status, etc.)
```

### Output Formats

```bash
harness task list              # Human-readable table
harness task list --json       # JSON for AI consumption
harness task list --quiet      # IDs only
```

### Exit Codes

- `0`: Success
- `1`: General error
- `2`: Not a harness project (no .harness/)
- `3`: Database error

---

## F006 Update: Project Setup

When HQ opens a directory:

| Scenario | Action |
|----------|--------|
| Has `.harness/harness.db` | Open normally |
| Has `.harness/` with TXT/JSON (legacy) | Show "Migrate to SQLite" button |
| No `.harness/` folder | Show "Initialize Harness" button |

Both buttons invoke equivalent of `harness init` or `harness migrate`.

---

## Implementation Order

### Phase 1: Core Storage (Rust)
1. Add `rusqlite` dependency with FTS5 feature
2. Create `src-tauri/src/db/mod.rs` - DB module
3. Create `src-tauri/src/db/schema.rs` - Schema initialization
4. Create `src-tauri/src/db/tasks.rs` - Task CRUD + FTS search
5. Create `src-tauri/src/db/progress.rs` - Progress CRUD + FTS search
6. Create `src-tauri/src/db/sessions.rs` - Session management
7. Create `src-tauri/src/db/pagination.rs` - Cursor pagination helpers

### Phase 2: CLI Binary
1. Create `src-tauri/src/bin/harness.rs` - CLI entry point
2. Add `clap` for argument parsing
3. Implement subcommands: init, task, step, progress, session, status
4. Add output formatters (table, json, quiet)

### Phase 3: Tauri Integration
1. Create Tauri commands that wrap DB operations
2. Update frontend to use new commands
3. Add project detection logic (has DB? has legacy? nothing?)

### Phase 4: Migration Tool
1. Create `harness migrate` command
2. Parse `features.json` → insert into `tasks` table
3. Parse `progress.txt` → insert into `progress` table
4. Preserve original files as `.harness/backup/`

---

## Files to Create

```
src-tauri/
├── src/
│   ├── bin/
│   │   └── harness.rs          # CLI entry point
│   ├── db/
│   │   ├── mod.rs              # Module exports
│   │   ├── schema.rs           # CREATE TABLE statements
│   │   ├── connection.rs       # DB connection management
│   │   ├── tasks.rs            # Task CRUD + search
│   │   ├── steps.rs            # Step CRUD
│   │   ├── progress.rs         # Progress CRUD + search
│   │   ├── sessions.rs         # Session management
│   │   ├── pagination.rs       # Cursor pagination
│   │   └── migrate.rs          # Legacy migration
│   └── lib.rs                  # Add db module
```

## Files to Modify

```
src-tauri/Cargo.toml            # Add rusqlite, clap
src-tauri/src/lib.rs            # Register db module + Tauri commands
src/components/KanbanBoard.tsx  # Use new Tauri commands (later)
.harness/features.json          # Add F013, F014 entries
```

## Dependencies to Add

```toml
# src-tauri/Cargo.toml
rusqlite = { version = "0.32", features = ["bundled", "fts5"] }
clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive"] }
```

---

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `harness init` creates `.harness/harness.db` with correct schema
- [ ] `harness task add/list/search/update/delete` work correctly
- [ ] `harness progress add/list/search` work correctly
- [ ] FTS5 search returns relevant results
- [ ] Pagination with prev/next cursors works
- [ ] `--json` output is valid JSON parseable by AI
- [ ] `harness migrate` successfully imports legacy TXT/JSON
- [ ] Tauri app can detect and handle all 3 project states

Plano criado em `/Users/anotonio.silva/.claude/plans/zesty-crafting-puppy.md`.

## Resumo do Plano

### Schema SQLite
- **tasks**: id, title, description, status (6 opções), category, `order` (REAL), passes, timestamps
- **task_steps**: id, task_id, description, completed, order_index
- **progress**: id, session_id, content, created_at
- **sessions**: id, started_at, ended_at, summary
- **FTS5**: tasks_fts, progress_fts com triggers de sync

### CLI `harness`
```bash
harness init                    # Setup
harness task add/list/search/update/delete
harness step add/complete/list
harness progress add/list/search
harness session start/end/list
harness status / harness db status
```

Output: `--json` para AI, `--quiet` para IDs, tabela por padrão.

### Fases de Implementação
1. **Core Storage** (Rust db module)
2. **CLI Binary** (clap + subcommands)
3. **Tauri Integration** (commands + frontend)
4. **Migration Tool** (legacy → SQLite)

---

Há algo que precisa ser ajustado no plano antes de iniciar a implementação?

message.content: Usar ULID para ids, não usar nenhum campo ID com autoincrement. Não usar numero para representar boleanos, para enums usar enums, para datas usar datas (ou isodate), enums devem ser em uppercase (DONE, DOING, etc), usar nomes semantigos (project_progress, não "progress", etc)... revise melhor, siga melhores práticas, ultrathink!

content.thinking:
O usuário quer que eu revise o plano com melhores práticas:

1. **ULID para IDs** - não autoincrement
2. **Não usar INTEGER para boolean** - usar formato apropriado
3. **Enums como enums** - não strings livres
4. **Datas como datas** - ISO 8601 ou formato apropriado
5. **Enums em UPPERCASE** - DONE, DOING, BACKLOG, etc.
6. **Nomes semânticos** - project_progress ao invés de progress

Deixe-me pensar nas melhores práticas para SQLite:

### SQLite Limitations vs Best Practices

SQLite não tem tipos nativos como PostgreSQL. Mas podemos:

1. **ULID**: Armazenar como TEXT (26 chars), sortable por timestamp
2. **Boolean**: SQLite só tem INTEGER, mas podemos usar CHECK constraints e nomear bem
3. **Enums**: TEXT com CHECK constraint listando valores válidos
4. **Datas**: TEXT em formato ISO 8601 (SQLite recomenda isso) - "2026-01-04T12:30:00Z"
5. **Nomes semânticos**:
   - `progress` → `project_progress`
   - `sessions` → `work_sessions`
   - `tasks` → `project_tasks`
   - `task_steps` → `task_steps` (já está bom)

### Sobre Booleans em SQLite

SQLite não tem tipo BOOLEAN nativo. As opções são:
- `INTEGER` com 0/1 (mais comum)
- `TEXT` com 'true'/'false' (mais legível mas menos eficiente)

A convenção SQLite é usar INTEGER 0/1, mas podemos melhorar a semântica com:
- Nomes claros: `is_completed`, `is_passed`


- Adicionar restrições para garantir valores válidos: `CHECK(is_completed IN (0, 1))`

### Formato de Data

Utilizando Text Affinity do SQLite para datas ISO 8601:
- Definir campo com valor padrão de data atual
- Manter formato consistente: "2026-01-04T15:30:00Z"

### Enums em UPPERCASE

Definir status com valores predefinidos em maiúsculas:
- Usar CHECK constraint para limitar valores
- Exemplo: status com opções como BACKLOG, TODO, DOING, REVIEW, QA, DONE

### ULID como Identificador Único

Vantagens do ULID sobre UUID:
- Ordenável por timestamp
- Mais compacto (26 caracteres)
- Compatível com URLs
- Simples de implementar em Rust

content.text: Vou revisar o plano com melhores práticas. Deixe-me analisar profundamente.

input.new_string:
# F013 + F014: SQLite Storage Architecture + CLI Utility

## Overview

Replace JSON/TXT files with SQLite + FTS5 for projects managed by HQ.
Provide a CLI utility (`harness`) for AI agents and humans to interact programmatically.

**Scope:** This is for projects that HQ will manage, NOT for HQ's own `.harness/` folder.

---

## F013: Storage Architecture

### Database Location
```
<project>/.harness/harness.db
```

### Design Decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|----------|--------|-----------|
| **Primary Keys** | ULID (TEXT 26 chars) | Sortable by timestamp, no coordination needed, URL-safe |
| **Timestamps** | ISO 8601 TEXT | Human-readable, sortable, SQLite datetime() compatible |
| **Booleans** | TEXT 'true'/'false' | Explicit semantics, JSON-compatible |
| **Enums** | UPPERCASE TEXT + CHECK | Clear constants, enforced at DB level |
| **Naming** | Semantic prefixes | `project_tasks`, `work_sessions` - clear domain context |

### Schema

```sql
-- ============================================================================
-- SCHEMA VERSION
-- ============================================================================
CREATE TABLE schema_metadata (
  key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  value TEXT NOT NULL
);
INSERT INTO schema_metadata (key, value) VALUES ('version', '1.0.0');

-- ============================================================================
-- ENUMS (documented as CHECK constraints)
-- ============================================================================
-- TaskStatus: BACKLOG | TODO | DOING | REVIEW | QA | DONE

-- ============================================================================
-- CORE TABLES
-- ============================================================================

-- Project Tasks (replaces features.json)
CREATE TABLE project_tasks (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY CHECK(length(id) = 26),           -- ULID
  title TEXT NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'BACKLOG'
    CHECK(status IN ('BACKLOG', 'TODO', 'DOING', 'REVIEW', 'QA', 'DONE')),
  category TEXT,
  sort_order REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.0,                 -- float for fractional ordering
  is_passed TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'false'
    CHECK(is_passed IN ('true', 'false')),
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL,                             -- ISO 8601: 2026-01-04T15:30:00Z
  updated_at TEXT NOT NULL                              -- ISO 8601
);

-- Task Steps (checklist items within a task)
CREATE TABLE task_steps (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY CHECK(length(id) = 26),           -- ULID
  task_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES project_tasks(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  description TEXT NOT NULL,
  is_completed TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'false'
    CHECK(is_completed IN ('true', 'false')),
  sort_order INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL
);

-- Work Sessions (AI/human work periods)
CREATE TABLE work_sessions (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY CHECK(length(id) = 26),           -- ULID
  summary TEXT,
  started_at TEXT NOT NULL,                             -- ISO 8601
  ended_at TEXT                                         -- ISO 8601, NULL if ongoing
);

-- Project Progress (session log entries, replaces progress.txt)
CREATE TABLE project_progress (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY CHECK(length(id) = 26),           -- ULID
  session_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES work_sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  content TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL                              -- ISO 8601
);

-- ============================================================================
-- FULL-TEXT SEARCH (FTS5)
-- ============================================================================

CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE project_tasks_fts USING fts5(
  title,
  description,
  category,
  content='project_tasks',
  content_rowid='rowid'
);

CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE project_progress_fts USING fts5(
  content,
  content='project_progress',
  content_rowid='rowid'
);

-- ============================================================================
-- FTS SYNC TRIGGERS
-- ============================================================================

-- project_tasks -> project_tasks_fts
CREATE TRIGGER project_tasks_fts_insert AFTER INSERT ON project_tasks BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_tasks_fts(rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.title, NEW.description, NEW.category);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER project_tasks_fts_delete AFTER DELETE ON project_tasks BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_tasks_fts(project_tasks_fts, rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.title, OLD.description, OLD.category);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER project_tasks_fts_update AFTER UPDATE ON project_tasks BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_tasks_fts(project_tasks_fts, rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.title, OLD.description, OLD.category);
  INSERT INTO project_tasks_fts(rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.title, NEW.description, NEW.category);
END;

-- project_progress -> project_progress_fts
CREATE TRIGGER project_progress_fts_insert AFTER INSERT ON project_progress BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_progress_fts(rowid, content)
  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.content);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER project_progress_fts_delete AFTER DELETE ON project_progress BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_progress_fts(project_progress_fts, rowid, content)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.content);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER project_progress_fts_update AFTER UPDATE ON project_progress BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_progress_fts(project_progress_fts, rowid, content)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.content);
  INSERT INTO project_progress_fts(rowid, content)
  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.content);
END;

-- ============================================================================
-- INDEXES
-- ============================================================================

CREATE INDEX idx_project_tasks_status ON project_tasks(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_project_tasks_sort_order ON project_tasks(sort_order);
CREATE INDEX idx_project_tasks_created_at ON project_tasks(created_at);
CREATE INDEX idx_task_steps_task_id ON task_steps(task_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_project_progress_session_id ON project_progress(session_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_project_progress_created_at ON project_progress(created_at);
CREATE INDEX idx_work_sessions_started_at ON work_sessions(started_at);
```

### ULID Format

```
 01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV
 |-------||------------|
 Timestamp   Randomness
 (48-bit)    (80-bit)
```

- **Lexicographically sortable** by creation time
- **26 characters**, case-insensitive (Crockford Base32)
- **No coordination** needed (unlike auto-increment)
- **URL-safe** (no special characters)

# F013 + F014: SQLite Storage Architecture + CLI Utility

## Overview

Replace JSON/TXT files with SQLite + FTS5 for projects managed by HQ.
Provide a CLI utility (`harness`) for AI agents and humans to interact programmatically.

**Scope:** This is for projects that HQ will manage, NOT for HQ's own `.harness/` folder.

---

## F013: Storage Architecture

### Database Location
```
<project>/.harness/harness.db
```

### Design Decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|----------|--------|-----------|
| **Primary Keys** | ULID (TEXT 26 chars) | Sortable by timestamp, no coordination needed, URL-safe |
| **Timestamps** | ISO 8601 TEXT | Human-readable, sortable, SQLite datetime() compatible |
| **Booleans** | TEXT 'true'/'false' | Explicit semantics, JSON-compatible |
| **Enums** | UPPERCASE TEXT + CHECK | Clear constants, enforced at DB level |
| **Naming** | Semantic prefixes | `project_tasks`, `work_sessions` - clear domain context |

### Schema

```sql
-- ============================================================================
-- SCHEMA VERSION
-- ============================================================================
CREATE TABLE schema_metadata (
  key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  value TEXT NOT NULL
);
INSERT INTO schema_metadata (key, value) VALUES ('version', '1.0.0');

-- ============================================================================
-- ENUMS (documented as CHECK constraints)
-- ============================================================================
-- TaskStatus: BACKLOG | TODO | DOING | REVIEW | QA | DONE

-- ============================================================================
-- CORE TABLES
-- ============================================================================

-- Project Tasks (replaces features.json)
CREATE TABLE project_tasks (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY CHECK(length(id) = 26),           -- ULID
  title TEXT NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'BACKLOG'
    CHECK(status IN ('BACKLOG', 'TODO', 'DOING', 'REVIEW', 'QA', 'DONE')),
  category TEXT,
  sort_order REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.0,                 -- float for fractional ordering
  is_passed TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'false'
    CHECK(is_passed IN ('true', 'false')),
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL,                             -- ISO 8601: 2026-01-04T15:30:00Z
  updated_at TEXT NOT NULL                              -- ISO 8601
);

-- Task Steps (checklist items within a task)
CREATE TABLE task_steps (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY CHECK(length(id) = 26),           -- ULID
  task_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES project_tasks(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  description TEXT NOT NULL,
  is_completed TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'false'
    CHECK(is_completed IN ('true', 'false')),
  sort_order INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL
);

-- Work Sessions (AI/human work periods)
CREATE TABLE work_sessions (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY CHECK(length(id) = 26),           -- ULID
  summary TEXT,
  started_at TEXT NOT NULL,                             -- ISO 8601
  ended_at TEXT                                         -- ISO 8601, NULL if ongoing
);

-- Project Progress (session log entries, replaces progress.txt)
CREATE TABLE project_progress (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY CHECK(length(id) = 26),           -- ULID
  session_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES work_sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  content TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL                              -- ISO 8601
);

-- ============================================================================
-- FULL-TEXT SEARCH (FTS5)
-- ============================================================================

CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE project_tasks_fts USING fts5(
  title,
  description,
  category,
  content='project_tasks',
  content_rowid='rowid'
);

CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE project_progress_fts USING fts5(
  content,
  content='project_progress',
  content_rowid='rowid'
);

-- ============================================================================
-- FTS SYNC TRIGGERS
-- ============================================================================

-- project_tasks -> project_tasks_fts
CREATE TRIGGER project_tasks_fts_insert AFTER INSERT ON project_tasks BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_tasks_fts(rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.title, NEW.description, NEW.category);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER project_tasks_fts_delete AFTER DELETE ON project_tasks BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_tasks_fts(project_tasks_fts, rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.title, OLD.description, OLD.category);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER project_tasks_fts_update AFTER UPDATE ON project_tasks BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_tasks_fts(project_tasks_fts, rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.title, OLD.description, OLD.category);
  INSERT INTO project_tasks_fts(rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.title, NEW.description, NEW.category);
END;

-- project_progress -> project_progress_fts
CREATE TRIGGER project_progress_fts_insert AFTER INSERT ON project_progress BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_progress_fts(rowid, content)
  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.content);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER project_progress_fts_delete AFTER DELETE ON project_progress BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_progress_fts(project_progress_fts, rowid, content)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.content);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER project_progress_fts_update AFTER UPDATE ON project_progress BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_progress_fts(project_progress_fts, rowid, content)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.content);
  INSERT INTO project_progress_fts(rowid, content)
  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.content);
END;

-- ============================================================================
-- INDEXES
-- ============================================================================

CREATE INDEX idx_project_tasks_status ON project_tasks(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_project_tasks_sort_order ON project_tasks(sort_order);
CREATE INDEX idx_project_tasks_created_at ON project_tasks(created_at);
CREATE INDEX idx_task_steps_task_id ON task_steps(task_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_project_progress_session_id ON project_progress(session_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_project_progress_created_at ON project_progress(created_at);
CREATE INDEX idx_work_sessions_started_at ON work_sessions(started_at);
```

### ULID Format

```
 01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV
 |-------||------------|
 Timestamp   Randomness
 (48-bit)    (80-bit)
```

- **Lexicographically sortable** by creation time
- **26 characters**, case-insensitive (Crockford Base32)
- **No coordination** needed (unlike auto-increment)
- **URL-safe** (no special characters)
toolUseResult.originalFile:
# F013 + F014: SQLite Storage Architecture + CLI Utility

## Overview

Replace JSON/TXT files with SQLite + FTS5 for projects managed by HQ.
Provide a CLI utility (`harness`) for AI agents and humans to interact programmatically.

**Scope:** This is for projects that HQ will manage, NOT for HQ's own `.harness/` folder.

---

## F013: Storage Architecture

### Database Location
```
<project>/.harness/harness.db
```

### Schema

```sql
-- Tasks (replaces features.json)
CREATE TABLE tasks (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  title TEXT NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'backlog'
    CHECK(status IN ('backlog', 'todo', 'doing', 'review', 'qa', 'done')),
  category TEXT,
  "order" REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.0,  -- float for flexible ordering
  passes INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,   -- boolean: 0 or 1
  created_at INTEGER NOT NULL,         -- unix timestamp
  updated_at INTEGER NOT NULL          -- unix timestamp
);

-- Task steps (child of tasks)
CREATE TABLE task_steps (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  task_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  description TEXT NOT NULL,
  completed INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  order_index INTEGER NOT NULL
);

-- Progress log (replaces progress.txt)
CREATE TABLE progress (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  session_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  content TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at INTEGER NOT NULL
);

-- Sessions
CREATE TABLE sessions (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  started_at INTEGER NOT NULL,
  ended_at INTEGER,
  summary TEXT
);

-- FTS5 virtual tables for full-text search
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE tasks_fts USING fts5(
  title, description, category,
  content='tasks',
  content_rowid='rowid'
);

CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE progress_fts USING fts5(
  content,
  content='progress',
  content_rowid='id'
);

-- Triggers to keep FTS in sync
CREATE TRIGGER tasks_ai AFTER INSERT ON tasks BEGIN
  INSERT INTO tasks_fts(rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.title, NEW.description, NEW.category);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER tasks_ad AFTER DELETE ON tasks BEGIN
  INSERT INTO tasks_fts(tasks_fts, rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.title, OLD.description, OLD.category);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER tasks_au AFTER UPDATE ON tasks BEGIN
  INSERT INTO tasks_fts(tasks_fts, rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.title, OLD.description, OLD.category);
  INSERT INTO tasks_fts(rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.title, NEW.description, NEW.category);
END;

-- Same triggers for progress_fts...
CREATE TRIGGER progress_ai AFTER INSERT ON progress BEGIN
  INSERT INTO progress_fts(rowid, content) VALUES (NEW.id, NEW.content);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER progress_ad AFTER DELETE ON progress BEGIN
  INSERT INTO progress_fts(progress_fts, rowid, content)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.id, OLD.content);
END;

-- Indexes
CREATE INDEX idx_tasks_status ON tasks(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_tasks_order ON tasks("order");
CREATE INDEX idx_progress_session ON progress(session_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_progress_created ON progress(created_at);
```

### Pagination Model

All list methods return:
```typescript
interface PaginatedResult<T> {
  items: T[];
  hasNext: boolean;
  hasPrev: boolean;
  nextCursor: string | null;  // base64 encoded cursor
  prevCursor: string | null;
}
```

Cursor-based using `id` or `created_at` for stable pagination.

---

## F014: CLI Utility (`harness`)

### Binary Structure

Add to `src-tauri/Cargo.toml`:
```toml
[[bin]]
name = "harness"
path = "src/bin/harness.rs"
```

Shared library code in `src-tauri/src/db/` used by both Tauri app and CLI.

### Commands

```bash
# Setup
harness init                              # Create .harness/harness.db
harness migrate                           # Migrate from legacy TXT/JSON
harness db status                         # Show DB info (size, counts)

# Tasks
harness task add "Title" [-d "desc"] [-c category] [-s status]
harness task list [--status=X] [--limit=N] [--cursor=X]
harness task search "query" [--limit=N] [--cursor=X]
harness task get <id>
harness task update <id> [--status=X] [--title=X] [--desc=X] [--order=X]
harness task delete <id>
harness task reorder <id> --after=<other_id>  # Reposition in order

# Task Steps
harness step add <task_id> "description"
harness step complete <step_id>
harness step uncomplete <step_id>
harness step list <task_id>
harness step delete <step_id>

# Progress
harness progress add "content" [--session=X]
harness progress list [--limit=N] [--cursor=X]
harness progress search "query" [--limit=N] [--cursor=X]

# Sessions
harness session start [--summary="X"]
harness session end [--summary="X"]
harness session list [--limit=N]
harness session current

# Info
harness status                            # Project summary (tasks by status, etc.)
```

### Output Formats

```bash
harness task list              # Human-readable table
harness task list --json       # JSON for AI consumption
harness task list --quiet      # IDs only
```

### Exit Codes

- `0`: Success
- `1`: General error
- `2`: Not a harness project (no .harness/)
- `3`: Database error

---

## F006 Update: Project Setup

When HQ opens a directory:

| Scenario | Action |
|----------|--------|
| Has `.harness/harness.db` | Open normally |
| Has `.harness/` with TXT/JSON (legacy) | Show "Migrate to SQLite" button |
| No `.harness/` folder | Show "Initialize Harness" button |

Both buttons invoke equivalent of `harness init` or `harness migrate`.

---

## Implementation Order

### Phase 1: Core Storage (Rust)
1. Add `rusqlite` dependency with FTS5 feature
2. Create `src-tauri/src/db/mod.rs` - DB module
3. Create `src-tauri/src/db/schema.rs` - Schema initialization
4. Create `src-tauri/src/db/tasks.rs` - Task CRUD + FTS search
5. Create `src-tauri/src/db/progress.rs` - Progress CRUD + FTS search
6. Create `src-tauri/src/db/sessions.rs` - Session management
7. Create `src-tauri/src/db/pagination.rs` - Cursor pagination helpers

### Phase 2: CLI Binary
1. Create `src-tauri/src/bin/harness.rs` - CLI entry point
2. Add `clap` for argument parsing
3. Implement subcommands: init, task, step, progress, session, status
4. Add output formatters (table, json, quiet)

### Phase 3: Tauri Integration
1. Create Tauri commands that wrap DB operations
2. Update frontend to use new commands
3. Add project detection logic (has DB? has legacy? nothing?)

### Phase 4: Migration Tool
1. Create `harness migrate` command
2. Parse `features.json` → insert into `tasks` table
3. Parse `progress.txt` → insert into `progress` table
4. Preserve original files as `.harness/backup/`

---

## Files to Create

```
src-tauri/
├── src/
│   ├── bin/
│   │   └── harness.rs          # CLI entry point
│   ├── db/
│   │   ├── mod.rs              # Module exports
│   │   ├── schema.rs           # CREATE TABLE statements
│   │   ├── connection.rs       # DB connection management
│   │   ├── tasks.rs            # Task CRUD + search
│   │   ├── steps.rs            # Step CRUD
│   │   ├── progress.rs         # Progress CRUD + search
│   │   ├── sessions.rs         # Session management
│   │   ├── pagination.rs       # Cursor pagination
│   │   └── migrate.rs          # Legacy migration
│   └── lib.rs                  # Add db module
```

## Files to Modify

```
src-tauri/Cargo.toml            # Add rusqlite, clap
src-tauri/src/lib.rs            # Register db module + Tauri commands
src/components/KanbanBoard.tsx  # Use new Tauri commands (later)
.harness/features.json          # Add F013, F014 entries
```

## Dependencies to Add

```toml
# src-tauri/Cargo.toml
rusqlite = { version = "0.32", features = ["bundled", "fts5"] }
clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive"] }
```

---

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `harness init` creates `.harness/harness.db` with correct schema
- [ ] `harness task add/list/search/update/delete` work correctly
- [ ] `harness progress add/list/search` work correctly
- [ ] FTS5 search returns relevant results
- [ ] Pagination with prev/next cursors works
- [ ] `--json` output is valid JSON parseable by AI
- [ ] `harness migrate` successfully imports legacy TXT/JSON
- [ ] Tauri app can detect and handle all 3 project states

lines: +| **Primary Keys** | ULID (TEXT 26 chars) | Sortable by timestamp, no coordination needed, URL-safe |
+| **Timestamps** | ISO 8601 TEXT | Human-readable, sortable, SQLite datetime() compatible |
+| **Enums** | UPPERCASE TEXT + CHECK | Clear constants, enforced at DB level |

## Dependencies to Add

```toml

rusqlite = { version = "0.32", features = ["bundled", "fts5"] }
clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive"] }
ulid = "1"                    # ULID generation

```

### Rust Types (Enums)

```rust
// src-tauri/src/db/types.rs

use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
#[serde(rename_all = "SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE")]
pub enum TaskStatus {
    Backlog,
    Todo,
    Doing,
    Review,
    Qa,
    Done,
}

impl TaskStatus {
    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {
        match self {
            Self::Backlog => "BACKLOG",
            Self::Todo => "TODO",
            Self::Doing => "DOING",
            Self::Review => "REVIEW",
            Self::Qa => "QA",
            Self::Done => "DONE",
        }
    }
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ProjectTask {
    pub id: String,                    // ULID
    pub title: String,
    pub description: Option<String>,
    pub status: TaskStatus,
    pub category: Option<String>,
    pub sort_order: f64,
    pub is_passed: bool,               // Rust bool, serialized as "true"/"false"
    pub created_at: String,            // ISO 8601
    pub updated_at: String,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct TaskStep {
    pub id: String,                    // ULID
    pub task_id: String,
    pub description: String,
    pub is_completed: bool,
    pub sort_order: i32,
    pub created_at: String,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct WorkSession {
    pub id: String,                    // ULID
    pub summary: Option<String>,
    pub started_at: String,
    pub ended_at: Option<String>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ProjectProgress {
    pub id: String,                    // ULID
    pub session_id: String,
    pub content: String,
    pub created_at: String,
}
```
