content.content:
1→# F013 + F014: SQLite Storage Architecture + CLI Utility
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→
     5→Replace JSON/TXT files with SQLite + FTS5 for projects managed by HQ.
     6→Provide a CLI utility (`harness`) for AI agents and humans to interact programmatically.
     7→
     8→**Scope:** This is for projects that HQ will manage, NOT for HQ's own `.harness/` folder.
     9→
    10→---
    11→
    12→## F013: Storage Architecture
    13→
    14→### Database Location
    15→```
    16→<project>/.harness/harness.db
    17→```
    18→
    19→### Design Decisions
    20→
    21→| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
    22→|----------|--------|-----------|
    23→| **Primary Keys** | ULID (TEXT 26 chars) | Sortable by timestamp, no coordination needed, URL-safe |
    24→| **Timestamps** | ISO 8601 TEXT | Human-readable, sortable, SQLite datetime() compatible |
    25→| **Booleans** | TEXT 'true'/'false' | Explicit semantics, JSON-compatible |
    26→| **Enums** | UPPERCASE TEXT + CHECK | Clear constants, enforced at DB level |
    27→| **Naming** | Semantic prefixes | `project_tasks`, `work_sessions` - clear domain context |
    28→
    29→### Schema
    30→
    31→```sql
    32→-- ============================================================================
    33→-- SCHEMA VERSION
    34→-- ============================================================================
    35→CREATE TABLE schema_metadata (
    36→  key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    37→  value TEXT NOT NULL
    38→);
    39→INSERT INTO schema_metadata (key, value) VALUES ('version', '1.0.0');
    40→
    41→-- ============================================================================
    42→-- ENUMS (documented as CHECK constraints)
    43→-- ============================================================================
    44→-- TaskStatus: BACKLOG | TODO | DOING | REVIEW | QA | DONE
    45→
    46→-- ============================================================================
    47→-- CORE TABLES
    48→-- ============================================================================
    49→
    50→-- Project Tasks (replaces features.json)
    51→CREATE TABLE project_tasks (
    52→  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY CHECK(length(id) = 26),           -- ULID
    53→  title TEXT NOT NULL,
    54→  description TEXT,
    55→  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'BACKLOG'
    56→    CHECK(status IN ('BACKLOG', 'TODO', 'DOING', 'REVIEW', 'QA', 'DONE')),
    57→  category TEXT,
    58→  sort_order REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.0,                 -- float for fractional ordering
    59→  is_passed TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'false'
    60→    CHECK(is_passed IN ('true', 'false')),
    61→  created_at TEXT NOT NULL,                             -- ISO 8601: 2026-01-04T15:30:00Z
    62→  updated_at TEXT NOT NULL                              -- ISO 8601
    63→);
    64→
    65→-- Task Steps (checklist items within a task)
    66→-- ID format: task#{task_ulid}#{5-digit-seq} → task#01ARZ3NDEK...#00001
    67→CREATE TABLE task_steps (
    68→  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,                                  -- Composite: task#{ulid}#{seq}
    69→  description TEXT NOT NULL,
    70→  is_completed TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'false'
    71→    CHECK(is_completed IN ('true', 'false')),
    72→  created_at TEXT NOT NULL
    73→);
    74→-- Query steps for task: WHERE id LIKE 'task#01ARZ...#%' ORDER BY id
    75→
    76→-- Work Sessions (AI/human work periods)
    77→CREATE TABLE work_sessions (
    78→  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY CHECK(length(id) = 26),           -- ULID
    79→  summary TEXT,
    80→  started_at TEXT NOT NULL,                             -- ISO 8601
    81→  ended_at TEXT                                         -- ISO 8601, NULL if ongoing
    82→);
    83→
    84→-- Project Progress (session log entries, replaces progress.txt)
    85→-- ID format: session#{session_ulid}#{progress_ulid}
    86→CREATE TABLE project_progress (
    87→  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,                                  -- Composite: session#{ulid}#{ulid}
    88→  content TEXT NOT NULL,
    89→  created_at TEXT NOT NULL                              -- ISO 8601
    90→);
    91→-- Query progress for session: WHERE id LIKE 'session#01ARZ...#%' ORDER BY id
    92→
    93→-- ============================================================================
    94→-- FULL-TEXT SEARCH (FTS5)
    95→-- ============================================================================
    96→
    97→CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE project_tasks_fts USING fts5(
    98→  title,
    99→  description,
   100→  category,
   101→  content='project_tasks',
   102→  content_rowid='rowid'
   103→);
   104→
   105→CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE project_progress_fts USING fts5(
   106→  content,
   107→  content='project_progress',
   108→  content_rowid='rowid'
   109→);
   110→
   111→-- ============================================================================
   112→-- FTS SYNC TRIGGERS
   113→-- ============================================================================
   114→
   115→-- project_tasks -> project_tasks_fts
   116→CREATE TRIGGER project_tasks_fts_insert AFTER INSERT ON project_tasks BEGIN
   117→  INSERT INTO project_tasks_fts(rowid, title, description, category)
   118→  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.title, NEW.description, NEW.category);
   119→END;
   120→
   121→CREATE TRIGGER project_tasks_fts_delete AFTER DELETE ON project_tasks BEGIN
   122→  INSERT INTO project_tasks_fts(project_tasks_fts, rowid, title, description, category)
   123→  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.title, OLD.description, OLD.category);
   124→END;
   125→
   126→CREATE TRIGGER project_tasks_fts_update AFTER UPDATE ON project_tasks BEGIN
   127→  INSERT INTO project_tasks_fts(project_tasks_fts, rowid, title, description, category)
   128→  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.title, OLD.description, OLD.category);
   129→  INSERT INTO project_tasks_fts(rowid, title, description, category)
   130→  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.title, NEW.description, NEW.category);
   131→END;
   132→
   133→-- project_progress -> project_progress_fts
   134→CREATE TRIGGER project_progress_fts_insert AFTER INSERT ON project_progress BEGIN
   135→  INSERT INTO project_progress_fts(rowid, content)
   136→  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.content);
   137→END;
   138→
   139→CREATE TRIGGER project_progress_fts_delete AFTER DELETE ON project_progress BEGIN
   140→  INSERT INTO project_progress_fts(project_progress_fts, rowid, content)
   141→  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.content);
   142→END;
   143→
   144→CREATE TRIGGER project_progress_fts_update AFTER UPDATE ON project_progress BEGIN
   145→  INSERT INTO project_progress_fts(project_progress_fts, rowid, content)
   146→  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.content);
   147→  INSERT INTO project_progress_fts(rowid, content)
   148→  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.content);
   149→END;
   150→
   151→-- ============================================================================
   152→-- INDEXES
   153→-- ============================================================================
   154→
   155→-- project_tasks indexes
   156→CREATE INDEX idx_project_tasks_status ON project_tasks(status);
   157→CREATE INDEX idx_project_tasks_sort_order ON project_tasks(sort_order);
   158→CREATE INDEX idx_project_tasks_created_at ON project_tasks(created_at);
   159→
   160→-- work_sessions indexes
   161→CREATE INDEX idx_work_sessions_started_at ON work_sessions(started_at);
   162→
   163→-- Note: task_steps and project_progress don't need FK indexes
   164→-- because parent lookup uses: WHERE id LIKE 'prefix#parentULID#%'
   165→-- SQLite PK index handles this efficiently with prefix matching
   166→```
   167→
   168→### ID Strategy
   169→
   170→#### Standalone Entities (ULID)
   171→```
   172→01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV
   173→|-------||------------|
   174→Timestamp   Randomness
   175→(48-bit)    (80-bit)
   176→```
   177→
   178→Used for: `project_tasks`, `work_sessions`
   179→
   180→#### Child Entities (Composite ID)
   181→```
   182→task#01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV#00001
   183→|---||--------------------------||---|
   184→Type      Parent ULID            Seq
   185→
   186→session#01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV#01BRZXYZ...
   187→|------||--------------------------||--------|
   188→Type        Parent ULID              Child ULID
   189→```
   190→
   191→Used for:
   192→- `task_steps`: `task#{task_ulid}#{5-digit seq}` → `task#01ARZ...#00001`
   193→- `project_progress`: `session#{session_ulid}#{progress_ulid}`
   194→
   195→#### Benefits
   196→- **No FK column needed** - parent ID embedded in child ID
   197→- **Prefix queries** - `WHERE id LIKE 'task#01ARZ...#%'` gets all steps
   198→- **Natural ordering** - sequence suffix sorts correctly
   199→- **Single index** - no composite index needed, just PK
   200→
   201→### Pagination Model
   202→
   203→All list methods return:
   204→```typescript
   205→interface PaginatedResult<T> {
   206→  items: T[];
   207→  hasNext: boolean;
   208→  hasPrev: boolean;
   209→  nextCursor: string | null;  // base64 encoded cursor
   210→  prevCursor: string | null;
   211→}
   212→```
   213→
   214→Cursor-based using `id` or `created_at` for stable pagination.
   215→
   216→---
   217→
   218→## F014: CLI Utility (`harness`)
   219→
   220→### Binary Structure
   221→
   222→Add to `src-tauri/Cargo.toml`:
   223→```toml
   224→[[bin]]
   225→name = "harness"
   226→path = "src/bin/harness.rs"
   227→```
   228→
   229→Shared library code in `src-tauri/src/db/` used by both Tauri app and CLI.
   230→
   231→### Commands
   232→
   233→```bash
   234→# Setup
   235→harness init                              # Create .harness/harness.db
   236→harness migrate                           # Migrate from legacy TXT/JSON
   237→harness db status                         # Show DB info (size, counts)
   238→
   239→# Tasks
   240→harness task add "Title" [-d "desc"] [-c category] [-s status]
   241→harness task list [--status=X] [--limit=N] [--cursor=X]
   242→harness task search "query" [--limit=N] [--cursor=X]
   243→harness task get <id>
   244→harness task update <id> [--status=X] [--title=X] [--desc=X] [--order=X]
   245→harness task delete <id>
   246→harness task reorder <id> --after=<other_id>  # Reposition in order
   247→
   248→# Task Steps
   249→harness step add <task_id> "description"
   250→harness step complete <step_id>
   251→harness step uncomplete <step_id>
   252→harness step list <task_id>
   253→harness step delete <step_id>
   254→
   255→# Progress
   256→harness progress add "content" [--session=X]
   257→harness progress list [--limit=N] [--cursor=X]
   258→harness progress search "query" [--limit=N] [--cursor=X]
   259→
   260→# Sessions
   261→harness session start [--summary="X"]
   262→harness session end [--summary="X"]
   263→harness session list [--limit=N]
   264→harness session current
   265→
   266→# Info
   267→harness status                            # Project summary (tasks by status, etc.)
   268→```
   269→
   270→### Output Formats
   271→
   272→```bash
   273→harness task list              # Human-readable table
   274→harness task list --json       # JSON for AI consumption
   275→harness task list --quiet      # IDs only
   276→```
   277→
   278→### Exit Codes
   279→
   280→- `0`: Success
   281→- `1`: General error
   282→- `2`: Not a harness project (no .harness/)
   283→- `3`: Database error
   284→
   285→---
   286→
   287→## F006 Update: Project Setup
   288→
   289→When HQ opens a directory:
   290→
   291→| Scenario | Action |
   292→|----------|--------|
   293→| Has `.harness/harness.db` | Open normally |
   294→| Has `.harness/` with TXT/JSON (legacy) | Show "Migrate to SQLite" button |
   295→| No `.harness/` folder | Show "Initialize Harness" button |
   296→
   297→Both buttons invoke equivalent of `harness init` or `harness migrate`.
   298→
   299→---
   300→
   301→## Implementation Order
   302→
   303→### Phase 1: Core Storage (Rust)
   304→1. Add `rusqlite` dependency with FTS5 feature
   305→2. Create `src-tauri/src/db/mod.rs` - DB module
   306→3. Create `src-tauri/src/db/schema.rs` - Schema initialization
   307→4. Create `src-tauri/src/db/tasks.rs` - Task CRUD + FTS search
   308→5. Create `src-tauri/src/db/progress.rs` - Progress CRUD + FTS search
   309→6. Create `src-tauri/src/db/sessions.rs` - Session management
   310→7. Create `src-tauri/src/db/pagination.rs` - Cursor pagination helpers
   311→
   312→### Phase 2: CLI Binary
   313→1. Create `src-tauri/src/bin/harness.rs` - CLI entry point
   314→2. Add `clap` for argument parsing
   315→3. Implement subcommands: init, task, step, progress, session, status
   316→4. Add output formatters (table, json, quiet)
   317→
   318→### Phase 3: Tauri Integration
   319→1. Create Tauri commands that wrap DB operations
   320→2. Update frontend to use new commands
   321→3. Add project detection logic (has DB? has legacy? nothing?)
   322→
   323→### Phase 4: Migration Tool
   324→1. Create `harness migrate` command
   325→2. Parse `features.json` → insert into `tasks` table
   326→3. Parse `progress.txt` → insert into `progress` table
   327→4. Preserve original files as `.harness/backup/`
   328→
   329→---
   330→
   331→## Files to Create
   332→
   333→```
   334→src-tauri/
   335→├── src/
   336→│   ├── bin/
   337→│   │   └── harness.rs          # CLI entry point
   338→│   ├── db/
   339→│   │   ├── mod.rs              # Module exports
   340→│   │   ├── types.rs            # TaskStatus enum, structs (ProjectTask, etc.)
   341→│   │   ├── schema.rs           # CREATE TABLE statements
   342→│   │   ├── connection.rs       # DB connection management
   343→│   │   ├── tasks.rs            # Task CRUD + FTS search
   344→│   │   ├── steps.rs            # Step CRUD
   345→│   │   ├── progress.rs         # Progress CRUD + FTS search
   346→│   │   ├── sessions.rs         # Session management
   347→│   │   ├── pagination.rs       # Cursor-based pagination
   348→│   │   └── migrate.rs          # Legacy TXT/JSON migration
   349→│   └── lib.rs                  # Add db module
   350→```
   351→
   352→## Files to Modify
   353→
   354→```
   355→src-tauri/Cargo.toml            # Add rusqlite, clap
   356→src-tauri/src/lib.rs            # Register db module + Tauri commands
   357→src/components/KanbanBoard.tsx  # Use new Tauri commands (later)
   358→.harness/features.json          # Add F013, F014 entries
   359→```
   360→
   361→## Dependencies to Add
   362→
   363→```toml
   364→# src-tauri/Cargo.toml
   365→rusqlite = { version = "0.32", features = ["bundled", "fts5"] }
   366→clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive"] }
   367→ulid = "1"                    # ULID generation
   368→chrono = { version = "0.4", features = ["serde"] }  # ISO 8601 timestamps
   369→```
   370→
   371→### Rust Types (Enums)
   372→
   373→```rust
   374→// src-tauri/src/db/types.rs
   375→
   376→use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
   377→
   378→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
   379→#[serde(rename_all = "SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE")]
   380→pub enum TaskStatus {
   381→    Backlog,
   382→    Todo,
   383→    Doing,
   384→    Review,
   385→    Qa,
   386→    Done,
   387→}
   388→
   389→impl TaskStatus {
   390→    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {
   391→        match self {
   392→            Self::Backlog => "BACKLOG",
   393→            Self::Todo => "TODO",
   394→            Self::Doing => "DOING",
   395→            Self::Review => "REVIEW",
   396→            Self::Qa => "QA",
   397→            Self::Done => "DONE",
   398→        }
   399→    }
   400→}
   401→
   402→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   403→pub struct ProjectTask {
   404→    pub id: String,                    // ULID
   405→    pub title: String,
   406→    pub description: Option<String>,
   407→    pub status: TaskStatus,
   408→    pub category: Option<String>,
   409→    pub sort_order: f64,
   410→    pub is_passed: bool,               // Rust bool, serialized as "true"/"false"
   411→    pub created_at: String,            // ISO 8601
   412→    pub updated_at: String,
   413→}
   414→
   415→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   416→pub struct TaskStep {
   417→    pub id: String,                    // Composite: task#{task_ulid}#{seq}
   418→    pub description: String,
   419→    pub is_completed: bool,
   420→    pub created_at: String,
   421→}
   422→
   423→impl TaskStep {
   424→    /// Extract parent task ULID from composite ID
   425→    pub fn task_id(&self) -> &str {
   426→        // id = "task#01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV#00001"
   427→        self.id.split('#').nth(1).unwrap_or("")
   428→    }
   429→}
   430→
   431→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   432→pub struct WorkSession {
   433→    pub id: String,                    // ULID
   434→    pub summary: Option<String>,
   435→    pub started_at: String,
   436→    pub ended_at: Option<String>,
   437→}
   438→
   439→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   440→pub struct ProjectProgress {
   441→    pub id: String,                    // Composite: session#{session_ulid}#{progress_ulid}
   442→    pub content: String,
   443→    pub created_at: String,
   444→}
   445→
   446→impl ProjectProgress {
   447→    /// Extract parent session ULID from composite ID
   448→    pub fn session_id(&self) -> &str {
   449→        // id = "session#01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV#01BRZXYZ..."
   450→        self.id.split('#').nth(1).unwrap_or("")
   451→    }
   452→}
   453→```
   454→
   455→---
   456→
   457→## Success Criteria
   458→
   459→- [ ] `harness init` creates `.harness/harness.db` with correct schema
   460→- [ ] `harness task add/list/search/update/delete` work correctly
   461→- [ ] `harness progress add/list/search` work correctly
   462→- [ ] FTS5 search returns relevant results
   463→- [ ] Pagination with prev/next cursors works
   464→- [ ] `--json` output is valid JSON parseable by AI
   465→- [ ] `harness migrate` successfully imports legacy TXT/JSON
   466→- [ ] Tauri app can detect and handle all 3 project states
   467→

# F013 + F014: SQLite Storage Architecture + CLI Utility

## Overview

Replace JSON/TXT files with SQLite + FTS5 for projects managed by HQ.
Provide a CLI utility (`harness`) for AI agents and humans to interact programmatically.

**Scope:** This is for projects that HQ will manage, NOT for HQ's own `.harness/` folder.

---

## F013: Storage Architecture

### Database Location
```
<project>/.harness/harness.db
```

### Design Decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|----------|--------|-----------|
| **Primary Keys** | ULID (TEXT 26 chars) | Sortable by timestamp, no coordination needed, URL-safe |
| **Timestamps** | ISO 8601 TEXT | Human-readable, sortable, SQLite datetime() compatible |
| **Booleans** | TEXT 'true'/'false' | Explicit semantics, JSON-compatible |
| **Enums** | UPPERCASE TEXT + CHECK | Clear constants, enforced at DB level |
| **Naming** | Semantic prefixes | `project_tasks`, `work_sessions` - clear domain context |

### Schema

```sql
-- ============================================================================
-- SCHEMA VERSION
-- ============================================================================
CREATE TABLE schema_metadata (
  key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  value TEXT NOT NULL
);
INSERT INTO schema_metadata (key, value) VALUES ('version', '1.0.0');

-- ============================================================================
-- ENUMS (documented as CHECK constraints)
-- ============================================================================
-- TaskStatus: BACKLOG | TODO | DOING | REVIEW | QA | DONE

-- ============================================================================
-- CORE TABLES
-- ============================================================================

-- Project Tasks (replaces features.json)
CREATE TABLE project_tasks (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY CHECK(length(id) = 26),           -- ULID
  title TEXT NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'BACKLOG'
    CHECK(status IN ('BACKLOG', 'TODO', 'DOING', 'REVIEW', 'QA', 'DONE')),
  category TEXT,
  sort_order REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.0,                 -- float for fractional ordering
  is_passed TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'false'
    CHECK(is_passed IN ('true', 'false')),
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL,                             -- ISO 8601: 2026-01-04T15:30:00Z
  updated_at TEXT NOT NULL                              -- ISO 8601
);

-- Task Steps (checklist items within a task)
-- ID format: task#{task_ulid}#{5-digit-seq} → task#01ARZ3NDEK...#00001
CREATE TABLE task_steps (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,                                  -- Composite: task#{ulid}#{seq}
  description TEXT NOT NULL,
  is_completed TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'false'
    CHECK(is_completed IN ('true', 'false')),
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL
);
-- Query steps for task: WHERE id LIKE 'task#01ARZ...#%' ORDER BY id

-- Work Sessions (AI/human work periods)
CREATE TABLE work_sessions (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY CHECK(length(id) = 26),           -- ULID
  summary TEXT,
  started_at TEXT NOT NULL,                             -- ISO 8601
  ended_at TEXT                                         -- ISO 8601, NULL if ongoing
);

-- Project Progress (session log entries, replaces progress.txt)
-- ID format: session#{session_ulid}#{progress_ulid}
CREATE TABLE project_progress (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,                                  -- Composite: session#{ulid}#{ulid}
  content TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL                              -- ISO 8601
);
-- Query progress for session: WHERE id LIKE 'session#01ARZ...#%' ORDER BY id

-- ============================================================================
-- FULL-TEXT SEARCH (FTS5)
-- ============================================================================

CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE project_tasks_fts USING fts5(
  title,
  description,
  category,
  content='project_tasks',
  content_rowid='rowid'
);

CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE project_progress_fts USING fts5(
  content,
  content='project_progress',
  content_rowid='rowid'
);

-- ============================================================================
-- FTS SYNC TRIGGERS
-- ============================================================================

-- project_tasks -> project_tasks_fts
CREATE TRIGGER project_tasks_fts_insert AFTER INSERT ON project_tasks BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_tasks_fts(rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.title, NEW.description, NEW.category);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER project_tasks_fts_delete AFTER DELETE ON project_tasks BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_tasks_fts(project_tasks_fts, rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.title, OLD.description, OLD.category);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER project_tasks_fts_update AFTER UPDATE ON project_tasks BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_tasks_fts(project_tasks_fts, rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.title, OLD.description, OLD.category);
  INSERT INTO project_tasks_fts(rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.title, NEW.description, NEW.category);
END;

-- project_progress -> project_progress_fts
CREATE TRIGGER project_progress_fts_insert AFTER INSERT ON project_progress BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_progress_fts(rowid, content)
  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.content);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER project_progress_fts_delete AFTER DELETE ON project_progress BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_progress_fts(project_progress_fts, rowid, content)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.content);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER project_progress_fts_update AFTER UPDATE ON project_progress BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_progress_fts(project_progress_fts, rowid, content)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.content);
  INSERT INTO project_progress_fts(rowid, content)
  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.content);
END;

-- ============================================================================
-- INDEXES
-- ============================================================================

-- project_tasks indexes
CREATE INDEX idx_project_tasks_status ON project_tasks(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_project_tasks_sort_order ON project_tasks(sort_order);
CREATE INDEX idx_project_tasks_created_at ON project_tasks(created_at);

-- work_sessions indexes
CREATE INDEX idx_work_sessions_started_at ON work_sessions(started_at);

-- Note: task_steps and project_progress don't need FK indexes
-- because parent lookup uses: WHERE id LIKE 'prefix#parentULID#%'
-- SQLite PK index handles this efficiently with prefix matching
```

### ID Strategy

#### Standalone Entities (ULID)
```
01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV
|-------||------------|
Timestamp   Randomness
(48-bit)    (80-bit)
```

Used for: `project_tasks`, `work_sessions`

#### Child Entities (Composite ID)
```
task#01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV#00001
|---||--------------------------||---|
Type      Parent ULID            Seq

session#01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV#01BRZXYZ...
|------||--------------------------||--------|
Type        Parent ULID              Child ULID
```

Used for:
- `task_steps`: `task#{task_ulid}#{5-digit seq}` → `task#01ARZ...#00001`
- `project_progress`: `session#{session_ulid}#{progress_ulid}`

#### Benefits
- **No FK column needed** - parent ID embedded in child ID
- **Prefix queries** - `WHERE id LIKE 'task#01ARZ...#%'` gets all steps
- **Natural ordering** - sequence suffix sorts correctly
- **Single index** - no composite index needed, just PK

### Pagination Model

All list methods return:
```typescript
interface PaginatedResult<T> {
  items: T[];
  hasNext: boolean;
  hasPrev: boolean;
  nextCursor: string | null;  // base64 encoded cursor
  prevCursor: string | null;
}
```

Cursor-based using `id` or `created_at` for stable pagination.

---

## F014: CLI Utility (`harness`)

### Binary Structure

Add to `src-tauri/Cargo.toml`:
```toml
[[bin]]
name = "harness"
path = "src/bin/harness.rs"
```

Shared library code in `src-tauri/src/db/` used by both Tauri app and CLI.

### Commands

```bash
# Setup
harness init                              # Create .harness/harness.db
harness migrate                           # Migrate from legacy TXT/JSON
harness db status                         # Show DB info (size, counts)

# Tasks
harness task add "Title" [-d "desc"] [-c category] [-s status]
harness task list [--status=X] [--limit=N] [--cursor=X]
harness task search "query" [--limit=N] [--cursor=X]
harness task get <id>
harness task update <id> [--status=X] [--title=X] [--desc=X] [--order=X]
harness task delete <id>
harness task reorder <id> --after=<other_id>  # Reposition in order

# Task Steps
harness step add <task_id> "description"
harness step complete <step_id>
harness step uncomplete <step_id>
harness step list <task_id>
harness step delete <step_id>

# Progress
harness progress add "content" [--session=X]
harness progress list [--limit=N] [--cursor=X]
harness progress search "query" [--limit=N] [--cursor=X]

# Sessions
harness session start [--summary="X"]
harness session end [--summary="X"]
harness session list [--limit=N]
harness session current

# Info
harness status                            # Project summary (tasks by status, etc.)
```

### Output Formats

```bash
harness task list              # Human-readable table
harness task list --json       # JSON for AI consumption
harness task list --quiet      # IDs only
```

### Exit Codes

- `0`: Success
- `1`: General error
- `2`: Not a harness project (no .harness/)
- `3`: Database error

---

## F006 Update: Project Setup

When HQ opens a directory:

| Scenario | Action |
|----------|--------|
| Has `.harness/harness.db` | Open normally |
| Has `.harness/` with TXT/JSON (legacy) | Show "Migrate to SQLite" button |
| No `.harness/` folder | Show "Initialize Harness" button |

Both buttons invoke equivalent of `harness init` or `harness migrate`.

---

## Implementation Order

### Phase 1: Core Storage (Rust)
1. Add `rusqlite` dependency with FTS5 feature
2. Create `src-tauri/src/db/mod.rs` - DB module
3. Create `src-tauri/src/db/schema.rs` - Schema initialization
4. Create `src-tauri/src/db/tasks.rs` - Task CRUD + FTS search
5. Create `src-tauri/src/db/progress.rs` - Progress CRUD + FTS search
6. Create `src-tauri/src/db/sessions.rs` - Session management
7. Create `src-tauri/src/db/pagination.rs` - Cursor pagination helpers

### Phase 2: CLI Binary
1. Create `src-tauri/src/bin/harness.rs` - CLI entry point
2. Add `clap` for argument parsing
3. Implement subcommands: init, task, step, progress, session, status
4. Add output formatters (table, json, quiet)

### Phase 3: Tauri Integration
1. Create Tauri commands that wrap DB operations
2. Update frontend to use new commands
3. Add project detection logic (has DB? has legacy? nothing?)

### Phase 4: Migration Tool
1. Create `harness migrate` command
2. Parse `features.json` → insert into `tasks` table
3. Parse `progress.txt` → insert into `progress` table
4. Preserve original files as `.harness/backup/`

## Dependencies to Add

rusqlite = { version = "0.32", features = ["bundled", "fts5"] }
clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive"] }
ulid = "1"                    # ULID generation

```

### Rust Types (Enums)

```rust
// src-tauri/src/db/types.rs

use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
#[serde(rename_all = "SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE")]
pub enum TaskStatus {
    Backlog,
    Todo,
    Doing,
    Review,
    Qa,
    Done,
}

impl TaskStatus {
    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {
        match self {
            Self::Backlog => "BACKLOG",
            Self::Todo => "TODO",
            Self::Doing => "DOING",
            Self::Review => "REVIEW",
            Self::Qa => "QA",
            Self::Done => "DONE",
        }
    }
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ProjectTask {
    pub id: String,                    // ULID
    pub title: String,
    pub description: Option<String>,
    pub status: TaskStatus,
    pub category: Option<String>,
    pub sort_order: f64,
    pub is_passed: bool,               // Rust bool, serialized as "true"/"false"
    pub created_at: String,            // ISO 8601
    pub updated_at: String,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct TaskStep {
    pub id: String,                    // Composite: task#{task_ulid}#{seq}
    pub description: String,
    pub is_completed: bool,
    pub created_at: String,
}

impl TaskStep {
    /// Extract parent task ULID from composite ID
    pub fn task_id(&self) -> &str {
        // id = "task#01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV#00001"
        self.id.split('#').nth(1).unwrap_or("")
    }
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct WorkSession {
    pub id: String,                    // ULID
    pub summary: Option<String>,
    pub started_at: String,
    pub ended_at: Option<String>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ProjectProgress {
    pub id: String,                    // Composite: session#{session_ulid}#{progress_ulid}
    pub content: String,
    pub created_at: String,
}

impl ProjectProgress {
    /// Extract parent session ULID from composite ID
    pub fn session_id(&self) -> &str {
        // id = "session#01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV#01BRZXYZ..."
        self.id.split('#').nth(1).unwrap_or("")
    }
}
```

---

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `harness init` creates `.harness/harness.db` with correct schema
- [ ] `harness task add/list/search/update/delete` work correctly
- [ ] `harness progress add/list/search` work correctly
- [ ] FTS5 search returns relevant results
- [ ] Pagination with prev/next cursors works
- [ ] `--json` output is valid JSON parseable by AI
- [ ] `harness migrate` successfully imports legacy TXT/JSON
- [ ] Tauri app can detect and handle all 3 project states

content.thinking:
Revisando o plano, identifico alguns pontos para melhorar:

1. **Inconsistência nos IDs compostos**: Os task_steps usam sequência numérica (00001) enquanto project_progress usa ULID. Deveria ser consistente.

2. **Falta de prefixo de created_at**: Para project_progress, usando `session#{session_ulid}#{progress_ulid}`, a ordenação natural vai ser pelo progress_ulid (que tem timestamp). Isso está OK.

3. **Falta PRAGMA para foreign keys**: SQLite precisa `PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON` para enforçar FK, mas como removemos FKs com IDs compostos, não é necessário.

4. **Problema com content_rowid='rowid'**: Em SQLite, quando você define TEXT PRIMARY KEY, não há rowid implícito (é WITHOUT ROWID table). Preciso verificar isso.

5. **FTS5 content sync**: Com tabelas WITHOUT ROWID (TEXT PK), precisamos usar uma abordagem diferente para FTS5.

Vou pesquisar sobre FTS5 com TEXT primary keys...

Na verdade, SQLite ainda cria um rowid interno mesmo com TEXT PRIMARY KEY, a menos que você especifique WITHOUT ROWID explicitamente. Então os triggers devem funcionar.

6. **CLI: falta de --next e --prev flags**: Para paginação cursor-based, precisa ter forma de passar o cursor.

7. **Falta de documentação sobre como steps mantêm ordering**: Se usamos seq 00001, 00002, etc., como fazemos reordenação? Precisamos de um campo sort_order separado ou aceitar que a ordem é append-only?

8. **task_steps sem sort_order**: Removemos o campo sort_order do task_steps, mas a sequência está no ID. Isso significa que não podemos reordenar steps - são sempre append-only. Isso é aceitável?


Implementarei as correções necessárias para padronizar a abordagem de IDs, garantir consistência na ordenação e definir claramente o comportamento de reordenação dos steps.

  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'BACKLOG'
    CHECK(status IN ('BACKLOG', 'TODO', 'DOING', 'REVIEW', 'QA', 'DONE')),
  category TEXT,
  sort_order REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.0,                 -- float for fractional ordering
  is_passed TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'false'
    CHECK(is_passed IN ('true', 'false')),
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL,                             -- ISO 8601: 2026-01-04T15:30:00Z
  updated_at TEXT NOT NULL                              -- ISO 8601
);

-- Task Steps (checklist items within a task)
-- ID format: step#{task_ulid}#{step_ulid} → step#01ARZ...#01BRZ...
-- Note: Steps are append-only, ordered by ULID timestamp. No reordering.
CREATE TABLE task_steps (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,                                  -- Composite: step#{task_ulid}#{step_ulid}
  description TEXT NOT NULL,
  is_completed TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'false'
    CHECK(is_completed IN ('true', 'false')),
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL
) WITHOUT ROWID;
-- Query steps for task: WHERE id GLOB 'step#01ARZ*' ORDER BY id

-- Work Sessions (AI/human work periods)
CREATE TABLE work_sessions (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY CHECK(length(id) = 26),           -- ULID
  summary TEXT,
  started_at TEXT NOT NULL,                             -- ISO 8601
  ended_at TEXT                                         -- ISO 8601, NULL if ongoing
);

-- Project Progress (session log entries, replaces progress.txt)
-- ID format: progress#{session_ulid}#{progress_ulid}
CREATE TABLE project_progress (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,                                  -- Composite: progress#{session_ulid}#{progress_ulid}
  content TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL                              -- ISO 8601
) WITHOUT ROWID;
-- Query progress for session: WHERE id GLOB 'progress#01ARZ*' ORDER BY id

# F013 + F014: SQLite Storage Architecture + CLI Utility

## Overview

Replace JSON/TXT files with SQLite + FTS5 for projects managed by HQ.
Provide a CLI utility (`harness`) for AI agents and humans to interact programmatically.

**Scope:** This is for projects that HQ will manage, NOT for HQ's own `.harness/` folder.

---

## F013: Storage Architecture

### Database Location
```
<project>/.harness/harness.db
```

### Design Decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|----------|--------|-----------|
| **Primary Keys** | ULID (TEXT 26 chars) | Sortable by timestamp, no coordination needed, URL-safe |
| **Timestamps** | ISO 8601 TEXT | Human-readable, sortable, SQLite datetime() compatible |
| **Booleans** | TEXT 'true'/'false' | Explicit semantics, JSON-compatible |
| **Enums** | UPPERCASE TEXT + CHECK | Clear constants, enforced at DB level |
| **Naming** | Semantic prefixes | `project_tasks`, `work_sessions` - clear domain context |

### Schema

```sql
-- ============================================================================
-- SCHEMA VERSION
-- ============================================================================
CREATE TABLE schema_metadata (
  key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  value TEXT NOT NULL
);
INSERT INTO schema_metadata (key, value) VALUES ('version', '1.0.0');

-- ============================================================================
-- ENUMS (documented as CHECK constraints)
-- ============================================================================
-- TaskStatus: BACKLOG | TODO | DOING | REVIEW | QA | DONE

-- ============================================================================
-- CORE TABLES
-- ============================================================================

-- Project Tasks (replaces features.json)
CREATE TABLE project_tasks (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY CHECK(length(id) = 26),           -- ULID
  title TEXT NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'BACKLOG'
    CHECK(status IN ('BACKLOG', 'TODO', 'DOING', 'REVIEW', 'QA', 'DONE')),
  category TEXT,
  sort_order REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.0,                 -- float for fractional ordering
  is_passed TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'false'
    CHECK(is_passed IN ('true', 'false')),
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL,                             -- ISO 8601: 2026-01-04T15:30:00Z
  updated_at TEXT NOT NULL                              -- ISO 8601
);

-- Task Steps (checklist items within a task)
-- ID format: task#{task_ulid}#{5-digit-seq} → task#01ARZ3NDEK...#00001
CREATE TABLE task_steps (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,                                  -- Composite: task#{ulid}#{seq}
  description TEXT NOT NULL,
  is_completed TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'false'
    CHECK(is_completed IN ('true', 'false')),
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL
);
-- Query steps for task: WHERE id LIKE 'task#01ARZ...#%' ORDER BY id

-- Work Sessions (AI/human work periods)
CREATE TABLE work_sessions (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY CHECK(length(id) = 26),           -- ULID
  summary TEXT,
  started_at TEXT NOT NULL,                             -- ISO 8601
  ended_at TEXT                                         -- ISO 8601, NULL if ongoing
);

-- Project Progress (session log entries, replaces progress.txt)
-- ID format: session#{session_ulid}#{progress_ulid}
CREATE TABLE project_progress (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,                                  -- Composite: session#{ulid}#{ulid}
  content TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL                              -- ISO 8601
);
-- Query progress for session: WHERE id LIKE 'session#01ARZ...#%' ORDER BY id

-- ============================================================================
-- FULL-TEXT SEARCH (FTS5)
-- ============================================================================

CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE project_tasks_fts USING fts5(
  title,
  description,
  category,
  content='project_tasks',
  content_rowid='rowid'
);

CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE project_progress_fts USING fts5(
  content,
  content='project_progress',
  content_rowid='rowid'
);

-- ============================================================================
-- FTS SYNC TRIGGERS
-- ============================================================================

-- project_tasks -> project_tasks_fts
CREATE TRIGGER project_tasks_fts_insert AFTER INSERT ON project_tasks BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_tasks_fts(rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.title, NEW.description, NEW.category);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER project_tasks_fts_delete AFTER DELETE ON project_tasks BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_tasks_fts(project_tasks_fts, rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.title, OLD.description, OLD.category);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER project_tasks_fts_update AFTER UPDATE ON project_tasks BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_tasks_fts(project_tasks_fts, rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.title, OLD.description, OLD.category);
  INSERT INTO project_tasks_fts(rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.title, NEW.description, NEW.category);
END;

-- project_progress -> project_progress_fts
CREATE TRIGGER project_progress_fts_insert AFTER INSERT ON project_progress BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_progress_fts(rowid, content)
  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.content);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER project_progress_fts_delete AFTER DELETE ON project_progress BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_progress_fts(project_progress_fts, rowid, content)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.content);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER project_progress_fts_update AFTER UPDATE ON project_progress BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_progress_fts(project_progress_fts, rowid, content)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.content);
  INSERT INTO project_progress_fts(rowid, content)
  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.content);
END;

-- ============================================================================
-- INDEXES
-- ============================================================================

-- project_tasks indexes
CREATE INDEX idx_project_tasks_status ON project_tasks(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_project_tasks_sort_order ON project_tasks(sort_order);
CREATE INDEX idx_project_tasks_created_at ON project_tasks(created_at);

-- work_sessions indexes
CREATE INDEX idx_work_sessions_started_at ON work_sessions(started_at);

-- Note: task_steps and project_progress don't need FK indexes
-- because parent lookup uses: WHERE id LIKE 'prefix#parentULID#%'
-- SQLite PK index handles this efficiently with prefix matching
```

### ID Strategy

#### Standalone Entities (ULID)
```
01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV
|-------||------------|
Timestamp   Randomness
(48-bit)    (80-bit)
```

Used for: `project_tasks`, `work_sessions`

#### Child Entities (Composite ID)
```
task#01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV#00001
|---||--------------------------||---|
Type      Parent ULID            Seq

session#01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV#01BRZXYZ...
|------||--------------------------||--------|
Type        Parent ULID              Child ULID
```

Used for:
- `task_steps`: `task#{task_ulid}#{5-digit seq}` → `task#01ARZ...#00001`
- `project_progress`: `session#{session_ulid}#{progress_ulid}`

#### Benefits
- **No FK column needed** - parent ID embedded in child ID
- **Prefix queries** - `WHERE id LIKE 'task#01ARZ...#%'` gets all steps
- **Natural ordering** - sequence suffix sorts correctly
- **Single index** - no composite index needed, just PK

### Pagination Model

All list methods return:
```typescript
interface PaginatedResult<T> {
  items: T[];
  hasNext: boolean;
  hasPrev: boolean;
  nextCursor: string | null;  // base64 encoded cursor
  prevCursor: string | null;
}
```

Cursor-based using `id` or `created_at` for stable pagination.

---

## F014: CLI Utility (`harness`)

### Binary Structure

Add to `src-tauri/Cargo.toml`:
```toml
[[bin]]
name = "harness"
path = "src/bin/harness.rs"
```

Shared library code in `src-tauri/src/db/` used by both Tauri app and CLI.

### Commands

```bash
# Setup
harness init                              # Create .harness/harness.db
harness migrate                           # Migrate from legacy TXT/JSON
harness db status                         # Show DB info (size, counts)

# Tasks
harness task add "Title" [-d "desc"] [-c category] [-s status]
harness task list [--status=X] [--limit=N] [--cursor=X]
harness task search "query" [--limit=N] [--cursor=X]
harness task get <id>
harness task update <id> [--status=X] [--title=X] [--desc=X] [--order=X]
harness task delete <id>
harness task reorder <id> --after=<other_id>  # Reposition in order

# Task Steps
harness step add <task_id> "description"
harness step complete <step_id>
harness step uncomplete <step_id>
harness step list <task_id>
harness step delete <step_id>

# Progress
harness progress add "content" [--session=X]
harness progress list [--limit=N] [--cursor=X]
harness progress search "query" [--limit=N] [--cursor=X]

# Sessions
harness session start [--summary="X"]
harness session end [--summary="X"]
harness session list [--limit=N]
harness session current

# Info
harness status                            # Project summary (tasks by status, etc.)
```

### Output Formats

```bash
harness task list              # Human-readable table
harness task list --json       # JSON for AI consumption
harness task list --quiet      # IDs only
```

### Exit Codes

- `0`: Success
- `1`: General error
- `2`: Not a harness project (no .harness/)
- `3`: Database error

---

## F006 Update: Project Setup

When HQ opens a directory:

| Scenario | Action |
|----------|--------|
| Has `.harness/harness.db` | Open normally |
| Has `.harness/` with TXT/JSON (legacy) | Show "Migrate to SQLite" button |
| No `.harness/` folder | Show "Initialize Harness" button |

Both buttons invoke equivalent of `harness init` or `harness migrate`.

---

## Implementation Order

### Phase 1: Core Storage (Rust)
1. Add `rusqlite` dependency with FTS5 feature
2. Create `src-tauri/src/db/mod.rs` - DB module
3. Create `src-tauri/src/db/schema.rs` - Schema initialization
4. Create `src-tauri/src/db/tasks.rs` - Task CRUD + FTS search
5. Create `src-tauri/src/db/progress.rs` - Progress CRUD + FTS search
6. Create `src-tauri/src/db/sessions.rs` - Session management
7. Create `src-tauri/src/db/pagination.rs` - Cursor pagination helpers

### Phase 2: CLI Binary
1. Create `src-tauri/src/bin/harness.rs` - CLI entry point
2. Add `clap` for argument parsing
3. Implement subcommands: init, task, step, progress, session, status
4. Add output formatters (table, json, quiet)

### Phase 3: Tauri Integration
1. Create Tauri commands that wrap DB operations
2. Update frontend to use new commands
3. Add project detection logic (has DB? has legacy? nothing?)

### Phase 4: Migration Tool
1. Create `harness migrate` command
2. Parse `features.json` → insert into `tasks` table
3. Parse `progress.txt` → insert into `progress` table
4. Preserve original files as `.harness/backup/`

---

## Files to Create

```
src-tauri/
├── src/
│   ├── bin/
│   │   └── harness.rs          # CLI entry point
│   ├── db/
│   │   ├── mod.rs              # Module exports
│   │   ├── types.rs            # TaskStatus enum, structs (ProjectTask, etc.)
│   │   ├── schema.rs           # CREATE TABLE statements
│   │   ├── connection.rs       # DB connection management
│   │   ├── tasks.rs            # Task CRUD + FTS search
│   │   ├── steps.rs            # Step CRUD
│   │   ├── progress.rs         # Progress CRUD + FTS search
│   │   ├── sessions.rs         # Session management
│   │   ├── pagination.rs       # Cursor-based pagination
│   │   └── migrate.rs          # Legacy TXT/JSON migration
│   └── lib.rs                  # Add db module
```

## Files to Modify

```
src-tauri/Cargo.toml            # Add rusqlite, clap
src-tauri/src/lib.rs            # Register db module + Tauri commands
src/components/KanbanBoard.tsx  # Use new Tauri commands (later)
.harness/features.json          # Add F013, F014 entries
```

## Dependencies to Add

```toml
# src-tauri/Cargo.toml
rusqlite = { version = "0.32", features = ["bundled", "fts5"] }
clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive"] }
ulid = "1"                    # ULID generation
chrono = { version = "0.4", features = ["serde"] }  # ISO 8601 timestamps
```

### Rust Types (Enums)

```rust
// src-tauri/src/db/types.rs

use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
#[serde(rename_all = "SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE")]
pub enum TaskStatus {
    Backlog,
    Todo,
    Doing,
    Review,
    Qa,
    Done,
}

impl TaskStatus {
    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {
        match self {
            Self::Backlog => "BACKLOG",
            Self::Todo => "TODO",
            Self::Doing => "DOING",
            Self::Review => "REVIEW",
            Self::Qa => "QA",
            Self::Done => "DONE",
        }
    }
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ProjectTask {
    pub id: String,                    // ULID
    pub title: String,
    pub description: Option<String>,
    pub status: TaskStatus,
    pub category: Option<String>,
    pub sort_order: f64,
    pub is_passed: bool,               // Rust bool, serialized as "true"/"false"
    pub created_at: String,            // ISO 8601
    pub updated_at: String,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct TaskStep {
    pub id: String,                    // Composite: task#{task_ulid}#{seq}
    pub description: String,
    pub is_completed: bool,
    pub created_at: String,
}

impl TaskStep {
    /// Extract parent task ULID from composite ID
    pub fn task_id(&self) -> &str {
        // id = "task#01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV#00001"
        self.id.split('#').nth(1).unwrap_or("")
    }
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct WorkSession {
    pub id: String,                    // ULID
    pub summary: Option<String>,
    pub started_at: String,
    pub ended_at: Option<String>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ProjectProgress {
    pub id: String,                    // Composite: session#{session_ulid}#{progress_ulid}
    pub content: String,
    pub created_at: String,
}

impl ProjectProgress {
    /// Extract parent session ULID from composite ID
    pub fn session_id(&self) -> &str {
        // id = "session#01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV#01BRZXYZ..."
        self.id.split('#').nth(1).unwrap_or("")
    }
}
```

---

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `harness init` creates `.harness/harness.db` with correct schema
- [ ] `harness task add/list/search/update/delete` work correctly
- [ ] `harness progress add/list/search` work correctly
- [ ] FTS5 search returns relevant results
- [ ] Pagination with prev/next cursors works
- [ ] `--json` output is valid JSON parseable by AI
- [ ] `harness migrate` successfully imports legacy TXT/JSON
- [ ] Tauri app can detect and handle all 3 project states

content.thinking:
Agora preciso atualizar o FTS para funcionar com tabelas WITHOUT ROWID. As tabelas WITHOUT ROWID não têm rowid implícito, então o FTS5 precisa usar uma abordagem diferente.

Para tabelas WITHOUT ROWID, não podemos usar `content_rowid`. Precisamos usar FTS5 em modo "contentless" ou repensar a estratégia.

Opções:
1. Remover WITHOUT ROWID das tabelas que precisam de FTS
2. Usar FTS5 contentless (armazena apenas o índice, não o conteúdo)
3. Usar uma coluna de ID numérica adicional

A melhor opção é remover WITHOUT ROWID apenas das tabelas que precisam de FTS (project_tasks e project_progress). Para task_steps não precisamos de FTS, então pode ficar WITHOUT ROWID.

Mas wait - project_tasks tem rowid porque não declarou WITHOUT ROWID. Apenas task_steps e project_progress têm WITHOUT ROWID.

Ah, mas project_progress precisa de FTS... Então preciso remover WITHOUT ROWID dela, ou usar FTS contentless.

Vou usar FTS5 external content mode com tabela que tem rowid para project_tasks (já tem), e para project_progress vou adicionar uma coluna rowid numérica OU remover WITHOUT ROWID.

Na verdade, a melhor abordagem é:
1. project_tasks - mantém com rowid implícito (não WITHOUT ROWID) → FTS funciona
2. project_progress - remover WITHOUT ROWID para ter rowid → FTS funciona
3. task_steps - manter WITHOUT ROWID (não precisa FTS)
4. work_sessions - não precisa FTS, pode ter rowid normal
5. schema_metadata - WITHOUT ROWID (pequena, não precisa FTS)


Implementarei essa solução imediatamente para garantir a compatibilidade do FTS.

# F013 + F014: SQLite Storage Architecture + CLI Utility

## Overview

Replace JSON/TXT files with SQLite + FTS5 for projects managed by HQ.
Provide a CLI utility (`harness`) for AI agents and humans to interact programmatically.

**Scope:** This is for projects that HQ will manage, NOT for HQ's own `.harness/` folder.

---

## F013: Storage Architecture

### Database Location
```
<project>/.harness/harness.db
```

### Design Decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|----------|--------|-----------|
| **Primary Keys** | ULID (TEXT 26 chars) | Sortable by timestamp, no coordination needed, URL-safe |
| **Timestamps** | ISO 8601 TEXT | Human-readable, sortable, SQLite datetime() compatible |
| **Booleans** | TEXT 'true'/'false' | Explicit semantics, JSON-compatible |
| **Enums** | UPPERCASE TEXT + CHECK | Clear constants, enforced at DB level |
| **Naming** | Semantic prefixes | `project_tasks`, `work_sessions` - clear domain context |

### Schema

```sql
-- ============================================================================
-- PRAGMAS (run on every connection)
-- ============================================================================
PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL;          -- Better concurrent access
PRAGMA synchronous = NORMAL;        -- Good balance of safety/speed
PRAGMA foreign_keys = OFF;          -- Using composite IDs, no FK enforcement needed

-- ============================================================================
-- SCHEMA VERSION
-- ============================================================================
CREATE TABLE schema_metadata (
  key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  value TEXT NOT NULL
) WITHOUT ROWID;
INSERT INTO schema_metadata (key, value) VALUES ('version', '1.0.0');

-- ============================================================================
-- ENUMS (documented as CHECK constraints)
-- ============================================================================
-- TaskStatus: BACKLOG | TODO | DOING | REVIEW | QA | DONE

-- ============================================================================
-- CORE TABLES
-- ============================================================================

-- Project Tasks (replaces features.json)
CREATE TABLE project_tasks (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY CHECK(length(id) = 26),           -- ULID
  title TEXT NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'BACKLOG'
    CHECK(status IN ('BACKLOG', 'TODO', 'DOING', 'REVIEW', 'QA', 'DONE')),
  category TEXT,
  sort_order REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.0,                 -- float for fractional ordering
  is_passed TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'false'
    CHECK(is_passed IN ('true', 'false')),
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL,                             -- ISO 8601: 2026-01-04T15:30:00Z
  updated_at TEXT NOT NULL                              -- ISO 8601
);

-- Task Steps (checklist items within a task)
-- ID format: step#{task_ulid}#{step_ulid} → step#01ARZ...#01BRZ...
-- Note: Steps are append-only, ordered by ULID timestamp. No reordering.
CREATE TABLE task_steps (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,                                  -- Composite: step#{task_ulid}#{step_ulid}
  description TEXT NOT NULL,
  is_completed TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'false'
    CHECK(is_completed IN ('true', 'false')),
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL
) WITHOUT ROWID;
-- Query steps for task: WHERE id GLOB 'step#01ARZ*' ORDER BY id

-- Work Sessions (AI/human work periods)
CREATE TABLE work_sessions (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY CHECK(length(id) = 26),           -- ULID
  summary TEXT,
  started_at TEXT NOT NULL,                             -- ISO 8601
  ended_at TEXT                                         -- ISO 8601, NULL if ongoing
);

-- Project Progress (session log entries, replaces progress.txt)
-- ID format: progress#{session_ulid}#{progress_ulid}
CREATE TABLE project_progress (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,                                  -- Composite: progress#{session_ulid}#{progress_ulid}
  content TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL                              -- ISO 8601
) WITHOUT ROWID;
-- Query progress for session: WHERE id GLOB 'progress#01ARZ*' ORDER BY id

-- ============================================================================
-- FULL-TEXT SEARCH (FTS5)
-- ============================================================================

CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE project_tasks_fts USING fts5(
  title,
  description,
  category,
  content='project_tasks',
  content_rowid='rowid'
);

CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE project_progress_fts USING fts5(
  content,
  content='project_progress',
  content_rowid='rowid'
);

-- ============================================================================
-- FTS SYNC TRIGGERS
-- ============================================================================

-- project_tasks -> project_tasks_fts
CREATE TRIGGER project_tasks_fts_insert AFTER INSERT ON project_tasks BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_tasks_fts(rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.title, NEW.description, NEW.category);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER project_tasks_fts_delete AFTER DELETE ON project_tasks BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_tasks_fts(project_tasks_fts, rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.title, OLD.description, OLD.category);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER project_tasks_fts_update AFTER UPDATE ON project_tasks BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_tasks_fts(project_tasks_fts, rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.title, OLD.description, OLD.category);
  INSERT INTO project_tasks_fts(rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.title, NEW.description, NEW.category);
END;

-- project_progress -> project_progress_fts
CREATE TRIGGER project_progress_fts_insert AFTER INSERT ON project_progress BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_progress_fts(rowid, content)
  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.content);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER project_progress_fts_delete AFTER DELETE ON project_progress BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_progress_fts(project_progress_fts, rowid, content)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.content);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER project_progress_fts_update AFTER UPDATE ON project_progress BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_progress_fts(project_progress_fts, rowid, content)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.content);
  INSERT INTO project_progress_fts(rowid, content)
  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.content);
END;

-- ============================================================================
-- INDEXES
-- ============================================================================

-- project_tasks indexes
CREATE INDEX idx_project_tasks_status ON project_tasks(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_project_tasks_sort_order ON project_tasks(sort_order);
CREATE INDEX idx_project_tasks_created_at ON project_tasks(created_at);

-- work_sessions indexes
CREATE INDEX idx_work_sessions_started_at ON work_sessions(started_at);

-- Note: task_steps and project_progress don't need FK indexes
-- because parent lookup uses: WHERE id LIKE 'prefix#parentULID#%'
-- SQLite PK index handles this efficiently with prefix matching
```

### ID Strategy

#### Standalone Entities (ULID)
```
01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV
|-------||------------|
Timestamp   Randomness
(48-bit)    (80-bit)
```

Used for: `project_tasks`, `work_sessions`

#### Child Entities (Composite ID)
```
task#01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV#00001
|---||--------------------------||---|
Type      Parent ULID            Seq

session#01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV#01BRZXYZ...
|------||--------------------------||--------|
Type        Parent ULID              Child ULID
```

Used for:
- `task_steps`: `task#{task_ulid}#{5-digit seq}` → `task#01ARZ...#00001`
- `project_progress`: `session#{session_ulid}#{progress_ulid}`

#### Benefits
- **No FK column needed** - parent ID embedded in child ID
- **Prefix queries** - `WHERE id LIKE 'task#01ARZ...#%'` gets all steps
- **Natural ordering** - sequence suffix sorts correctly
- **Single index** - no composite index needed, just PK

### Pagination Model

All list methods return:
```typescript
interface PaginatedResult<T> {
  items: T[];
  hasNext: boolean;
  hasPrev: boolean;
  nextCursor: string | null;  // base64 encoded cursor
  prevCursor: string | null;
}
```

Cursor-based using `id` or `created_at` for stable pagination.

---

## F014: CLI Utility (`harness`)

### Binary Structure

Add to `src-tauri/Cargo.toml`:
```toml
[[bin]]
name = "harness"
path = "src/bin/harness.rs"
```

Shared library code in `src-tauri/src/db/` used by both Tauri app and CLI.

### Commands

```bash
# Setup

# Tasks

harness task search "query" [--limit=N] [--cursor=X]

harness task update <id> [--status=X] [--title=X] [--desc=X] [--order=X]
harness task delete <id>
harness task reorder <id> --after=<other_id>  # Reposition in order

# Task Steps

harness step complete <step_id>
harness step uncomplete <step_id>
harness step list <task_id>
harness step delete <step_id>

# Progress

harness progress list [--limit=N] [--cursor=X]
harness progress search "query" [--limit=N] [--cursor=X]

# Sessions

harness session end [--summary="X"]
harness session list [--limit=N]
harness session current

# Info

```

### Output Formats

```bash
harness task list              # Human-readable table
harness task list --json       # JSON for AI consumption
harness task list --quiet      # IDs only
```

### Exit Codes

- `0`: Success
- `1`: General error
- `2`: Not a harness project (no .harness/)
- `3`: Database error

### Rust Types (Enums)

```rust
// src-tauri/src/db/types.rs

use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
#[serde(rename_all = "SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE")]
pub enum TaskStatus {
    Backlog,
    Todo,
    Doing,
    Review,
    Qa,
    Done,
}

impl TaskStatus {
    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {
        match self {
            Self::Backlog => "BACKLOG",
            Self::Todo => "TODO",
            Self::Doing => "DOING",
            Self::Review => "REVIEW",
            Self::Qa => "QA",
            Self::Done => "DONE",
        }
    }
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ProjectTask {
    pub id: String,                    // ULID
    pub title: String,
    pub description: Option<String>,
    pub status: TaskStatus,
    pub category: Option<String>,
    pub sort_order: f64,
    pub is_passed: bool,               // Rust bool, serialized as "true"/"false"
    pub created_at: String,            // ISO 8601
    pub updated_at: String,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct TaskStep {
    pub id: String,                    // Composite: task#{task_ulid}#{seq}
    pub description: String,
    pub is_completed: bool,
    pub created_at: String,
}

impl TaskStep {
    /// Extract parent task ULID from composite ID
    pub fn task_id(&self) -> &str {
        // id = "task#01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV#00001"
        self.id.split('#').nth(1).unwrap_or("")
    }
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct WorkSession {
    pub id: String,                    // ULID
    pub summary: Option<String>,
    pub started_at: String,
    pub ended_at: Option<String>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ProjectProgress {
    pub id: String,                    // Composite: session#{session_ulid}#{progress_ulid}
    pub content: String,
    pub created_at: String,
}

impl ProjectProgress {
    /// Extract parent session ULID from composite ID
    pub fn session_id(&self) -> &str {
        // id = "session#01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV#01BRZXYZ..."
        self.id.split('#').nth(1).unwrap_or("")
    }
}
```

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# F013 + F014: SQLite Storage Architecture + CLI Utility

## Overview

Replace JSON/TXT files with SQLite + FTS5 for projects managed by HQ.
Provide a CLI utility (`harness`) for AI agents and humans to interact programmatically.

**Scope:** This is for projects that HQ will manage, NOT for HQ's own `.harness/` folder.

---

## F013: Storage Architecture

### Database Location
```
<project>/.harness/harness.db
```

### Design Decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|----------|--------|-----------|
| **Primary Keys** | ULID (TEXT 26 chars) | Sortable by timestamp, no coordination needed, URL-safe |
| **Timestamps** | ISO 8601 TEXT | Human-readable, sortable, SQLite datetime() compatible |
| **Booleans** | TEXT 'true'/'false' | Explicit semantics, JSON-compatible |
| **Enums** | UPPERCASE TEXT + CHECK | Clear constants, enforced at DB level |
| **Naming** | Semantic prefixes | `project_tasks`, `work_sessions` - clear domain context |

### Schema

```sql
-- ============================================================================
-- PRAGMAS (run on every connection)
-- ============================================================================
PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL;          -- Better concurrent access
PRAGMA synchronous = NORMAL;        -- Good balance of safety/speed
PRAGMA foreign_keys = OFF;          -- Using composite IDs, no FK enforcement needed

-- ============================================================================
-- SCHEMA VERSION
-- ============================================================================
CREATE TABLE schema_metadata (
  key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  value TEXT NOT NULL
) WITHOUT ROWID;
INSERT INTO schema_metadata (key, value) VALUES ('version', '1.0.0');

-- ============================================================================
-- ENUMS (documented as CHECK constraints)
-- ============================================================================
-- TaskStatus: BACKLOG | TODO | DOING | REVIEW | QA | DONE

-- ============================================================================
-- CORE TABLES
-- ============================================================================

-- Project Tasks (replaces features.json)
CREATE TABLE project_tasks (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY CHECK(length(id) = 26),           -- ULID
  title TEXT NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'BACKLOG'
    CHECK(status IN ('BACKLOG', 'TODO', 'DOING', 'REVIEW', 'QA', 'DONE')),
  category TEXT,
  sort_order REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.0,                 -- float for fractional ordering
  is_passed TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'false'
    CHECK(is_passed IN ('true', 'false')),
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL,                             -- ISO 8601: 2026-01-04T15:30:00Z
  updated_at TEXT NOT NULL                              -- ISO 8601
);

-- Task Steps (checklist items within a task)
-- ID format: step#{task_ulid}#{step_ulid} → step#01ARZ...#01BRZ...
-- Note: Steps are append-only, ordered by ULID timestamp. No reordering.
CREATE TABLE task_steps (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,                                  -- Composite: step#{task_ulid}#{step_ulid}
  description TEXT NOT NULL,
  is_completed TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'false'
    CHECK(is_completed IN ('true', 'false')),
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL
) WITHOUT ROWID;
-- Query steps for task: WHERE id GLOB 'step#01ARZ*' ORDER BY id

-- Work Sessions (AI/human work periods)
CREATE TABLE work_sessions (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY CHECK(length(id) = 26),           -- ULID
  summary TEXT,
  started_at TEXT NOT NULL,                             -- ISO 8601
  ended_at TEXT                                         -- ISO 8601, NULL if ongoing
);

-- Project Progress (session log entries, replaces progress.txt)
-- ID format: progress#{session_ulid}#{progress_ulid}
-- Note: No WITHOUT ROWID - needs implicit rowid for FTS5 sync
CREATE TABLE project_progress (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,                                  -- Composite: progress#{session_ulid}#{progress_ulid}
  content TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL                              -- ISO 8601
);
-- Query progress for session: WHERE id GLOB 'progress#01ARZ*' ORDER BY id

-- ============================================================================
-- FULL-TEXT SEARCH (FTS5)
-- ============================================================================

CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE project_tasks_fts USING fts5(
  title,
  description,
  category,
  content='project_tasks',
  content_rowid='rowid'
);

CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE project_progress_fts USING fts5(
  content,
  content='project_progress',
  content_rowid='rowid'
);

-- ============================================================================
-- FTS SYNC TRIGGERS
-- ============================================================================

-- project_tasks -> project_tasks_fts
CREATE TRIGGER project_tasks_fts_insert AFTER INSERT ON project_tasks BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_tasks_fts(rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.title, NEW.description, NEW.category);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER project_tasks_fts_delete AFTER DELETE ON project_tasks BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_tasks_fts(project_tasks_fts, rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.title, OLD.description, OLD.category);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER project_tasks_fts_update AFTER UPDATE ON project_tasks BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_tasks_fts(project_tasks_fts, rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.title, OLD.description, OLD.category);
  INSERT INTO project_tasks_fts(rowid, title, description, category)
  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.title, NEW.description, NEW.category);
END;

-- project_progress -> project_progress_fts
CREATE TRIGGER project_progress_fts_insert AFTER INSERT ON project_progress BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_progress_fts(rowid, content)
  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.content);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER project_progress_fts_delete AFTER DELETE ON project_progress BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_progress_fts(project_progress_fts, rowid, content)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.content);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER project_progress_fts_update AFTER UPDATE ON project_progress BEGIN
  INSERT INTO project_progress_fts(project_progress_fts, rowid, content)
  VALUES('delete', OLD.rowid, OLD.content);
  INSERT INTO project_progress_fts(rowid, content)
  VALUES (NEW.rowid, NEW.content);
END;

-- ============================================================================
-- INDEXES
-- ============================================================================

-- project_tasks indexes
CREATE INDEX idx_project_tasks_status ON project_tasks(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_project_tasks_sort_order ON project_tasks(sort_order);
CREATE INDEX idx_project_tasks_created_at ON project_tasks(created_at);

-- work_sessions indexes
CREATE INDEX idx_work_sessions_started_at ON work_sessions(started_at);

-- Note: task_steps and project_progress don't need FK indexes
-- because parent lookup uses: WHERE id LIKE 'prefix#parentULID#%'
-- SQLite PK index handles this efficiently with prefix matching
```

### ID Strategy

#### Standalone Entities (ULID)
```
01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV
|-------||------------|
Timestamp   Randomness
(48-bit)    (80-bit)
```

Used for: `project_tasks`, `work_sessions`

#### Child Entities (Composite ID)
```
task#01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV#00001
|---||--------------------------||---|
Type      Parent ULID            Seq

session#01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV#01BRZXYZ...
|------||--------------------------||--------|
Type        Parent ULID              Child ULID
```

Used for:
- `task_steps`: `task#{task_ulid}#{5-digit seq}` → `task#01ARZ...#00001`
- `project_progress`: `session#{session_ulid}#{progress_ulid}`

#### Benefits
- **No FK column needed** - parent ID embedded in child ID
- **Prefix queries** - `WHERE id LIKE 'task#01ARZ...#%'` gets all steps
- **Natural ordering** - sequence suffix sorts correctly
- **Single index** - no composite index needed, just PK

### Pagination Model

All list methods return:
```typescript
interface PaginatedResult<T> {
  items: T[];
  hasNext: boolean;
  hasPrev: boolean;
  nextCursor: string | null;  // base64 encoded cursor
  prevCursor: string | null;
}
```

Cursor-based using `id` or `created_at` for stable pagination.

3. Add project detection logic (has DB? has legacy? nothing?)

### Phase 4: Migration Tool
1. Create `harness migrate` command
2. Parse `features.json` → insert into `tasks` table
3. Parse `progress.txt` → insert into `progress` table
4. Preserve original files as `.harness/backup/`

---

## Files to Create

```
src-tauri/
├── src/
│   ├── bin/
│   │   └── harness.rs          # CLI entry point
│   ├── db/
│   │   ├── mod.rs              # Module exports
│   │   ├── types.rs            # TaskStatus enum, structs (ProjectTask, etc.)
│   │   ├── schema.rs           # CREATE TABLE statements
│   │   ├── connection.rs       # DB connection management
│   │   ├── tasks.rs            # Task CRUD + FTS search
│   │   ├── steps.rs            # Step CRUD
│   │   ├── progress.rs         # Progress CRUD + FTS search
│   │   ├── sessions.rs         # Session management
│   │   ├── pagination.rs       # Cursor-based pagination
│   │   └── migrate.rs          # Legacy TXT/JSON migration
│   └── lib.rs                  # Add db module
```

## Files to Modify

```
src-tauri/Cargo.toml            # Add rusqlite, clap
src-tauri/src/lib.rs            # Register db module + Tauri commands
src/components/KanbanBoard.tsx  # Use new Tauri commands (later)
.harness/features.json          # Add F013, F014 entries
```

## Dependencies to Add

```toml

rusqlite = { version = "0.32", features = ["bundled", "fts5"] }
clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive"] }
ulid = "1"                    # ULID generation
chrono = { version = "0.4", features = ["serde"] }  # ISO 8601 timestamps
```

### Rust Types (Enums)

```rust
// src-tauri/src/db/types.rs

use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
#[serde(rename_all = "SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE")]
pub enum TaskStatus {
    Backlog,
    Todo,
    Doing,
    Review,
    Qa,
    Done,
}

impl TaskStatus {
    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {
        match self {
            Self::Backlog => "BACKLOG",
            Self::Todo => "TODO",
            Self::Doing => "DOING",
            Self::Review => "REVIEW",
            Self::Qa => "QA",
            Self::Done => "DONE",
        }
    }
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ProjectTask {
    pub id: String,                    // ULID
    pub title: String,
    pub description: Option<String>,
    pub status: TaskStatus,
    pub category: Option<String>,
    pub sort_order: f64,
    pub is_passed: bool,               // Rust bool, serialized as "true"/"false"
    pub created_at: String,            // ISO 8601
    pub updated_at: String,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct TaskStep {
    pub id: String,                    // Composite: task#{task_ulid}#{seq}
    pub description: String,
    pub is_completed: bool,
    pub created_at: String,
}

impl TaskStep {
    /// Extract parent task ULID from composite ID
    pub fn task_id(&self) -> &str {
        // id = "task#01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV#00001"
        self.id.split('#').nth(1).unwrap_or("")
    }
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct WorkSession {
    pub id: String,                    // ULID
    pub summary: Option<String>,
    pub started_at: String,
    pub ended_at: Option<String>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct ProjectProgress {
    pub id: String,                    // Composite: session#{session_ulid}#{progress_ulid}
    pub content: String,
    pub created_at: String,
}

impl ProjectProgress {
    /// Extract parent session ULID from composite ID
    pub fn session_id(&self) -> &str {
        // id = "session#01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV#01BRZXYZ..."
        self.id.split('#').nth(1).unwrap_or("")
    }
}
```

---

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `harness init` creates `.harness/harness.db` with correct schema
- [ ] `harness task add/list/search/update/delete` work correctly
- [ ] `harness progress add/list/search` work correctly

- [ ] Pagination with prev/next cursors works
- [ ] `--json` output is valid JSON parseable by AI
- [ ] `harness migrate` successfully imports legacy TXT/JSON
- [ ] Tauri app can detect and handle all 3 project states
